# Lynx

> Lynx is a leading cross-platform development tech stack to empower the web community to build cross-platform applications.

It is recognized within the Web community for:

- Speed, and Versatility: A strong alternative to other cross-platform solutions like Web, React Native, and Flutter, offering high performance, multi-platform coverage, multi-scenario adaptability, and flexible rendering backends.

- Platform Appeal: Welcoming existing frameworks and libraries from the Web into the field of cross-platform app development, fostering an ecosystem that inspires new patterns and innovations.

- Industry Impact: Driving innovation in practices across app development, micro-frontend, and UI frameworks and influencing open standards like W3C and ECMA.

## guide

- [Lynx: Glossary](https://lynxjs.org/guide/glossary): Describes technical terms in Lynx development; defines SDK (Engine and Framework), Lynx Platform components; helps developers understand Lynx - related concepts.
- [Lynx: Using Data from Host Platform](https://lynxjs.org/guide/use-data-from-host-platform): Provide and update data in Lynx apps; Consume data on frontend with React hooks or class components; Easy data processing with default or named data processors.
- [Lynx: Performance](https://lynxjs.org/guide/performance): Discusses making Lynx pages faster; focuses on maximizing runtime efficiency and fluid user experience, covering User Perceived and Application Runtime Performance; uses Performance API for measurement.
- [Guide: Embedding LynxView into Native View](https://lynxjs.org/guide/embed-lynx-to-native): How to embed LynxView in native views; supports full - screen or non - full - screen embedding; easy to set size constraints on LynxView for iOS and Android.
- [Lynx: Custom Element](https://lynxjs.org/guide/custom-native-component): Create custom native elements when built - in ones are insufficient; guides through building native code on Android, iOS, and web; easy to use custom elements like built - in ones.
- [Guide: Compatibility](https://lynxjs.org/guide/compatibility): Ensures version compatibility between Bundle and Lynx Engine; handles multi - version scenarios, new/deprecated features; use `engineVersion` in config to manage compatibility.
- [Guide: Living Specification](https://lynxjs.org/guide/spec): Presents a living specification; no specific core capabilities clearly stated; no ease - of - use statement provided.
- [Lynx: Native Modules](https://lynxjs.org/guide/use-native-modules): Connect JavaScript with native code; steps include declaring typed interface, writing Lynx app code, and native platform code; detailed type - mapping table aids in implementation.
- [Guide: Managing Scrolling](https://lynxjs.org/guide/ui/scrolling): Handle overflow in page building; crop overflow with `overflow` property, make content scrollable; use `<scroll-view>` for basic scroll, `<list>` for large data, complex layout; easy choice of dedicated scroll components.
- [Lynx: Composing Elements](https://lynxjs.org/guide/ui/elements-components): Learn to construct basic views; defines UI with element tags like <view>, <text>, <image>; uses attributes, supports nesting. Easy to understand for building Lynx pages.
- [Lynx: Styling with CSS](https://lynxjs.org/guide/ui/styling): Use CSS to style Lynx pages; set element properties via selectors, inline styles; supports nesting syntax for easier class declaration; beginner - friendly with helpful tips and external resources.
- [Guide: UI - Relative Layout](https://lynxjs.org/guide/ui/layout/relative-layout): Ideal for controlling relative positions between parent - children or siblings; enables layout with properties like relative - id, edge alignment, relative position, and center; offers a convenient and efficient layout experience with best practices.
- [Lynx: Understanding Layout](https://lynxjs.org/guide/ui/layout/index): Learn to create UI layouts; manage element sizing, use display properties for linear, flex, grid, relative layouts; easily align, offset, and position elements.
- [Lynx Guide: Flexible Box Layout](https://lynxjs.org/guide/ui/layout/flexible-box-layout): Make child elements adapt to parent space; core features include filling with `flex - grow`, shrinking with `flex - shrink`, and wrapping with `flex - wrap`; supports common flex layout properties as per Web standards.
- [Guide: Grid Layout](https://lynxjs.org/guide/ui/layout/grid-layout): Ideal for responsive vertical and horizontal element staggering; build via steps like setting `display: grid`, specifying row/column sizes, gaps, and item alignments; basic concepts make implementation straightforward.
- [Guide: UI Linear Layout](https://lynxjs.org/guide/ui/layout/linear-layout): Simplified sequential child arrangement; set display, direction, and alignment properties; easy to use for those avoiding flexbox and grid layout complexities.
- [Lynx: JavaScript Runtime](https://lynxjs.org/guide/scripting-runtime/index): Features dual - thread architecture with different engines per thread; supports specific ECMAScript versions, syntax transformers, and polyfills; allows use of ESModule and CommonJS for easy module - based development.
- [Guide: Main Thread Runtime](https://lynxjs.org/guide/scripting-runtime/main-thread-runtime): Utilizes PrimJS as runtime engine; offers high - performance FFI and Element PAPI bindings; uses bytecode format for scripts, four times faster than text, optimizing performance.
- [Guide: Accessibility](https://lynxjs.org/guide/inclusion/accessibility): Builds mobile app accessibility; core goal is to break usage barriers; Lynx framework encapsulates cross - platform interfaces; default behavior, tagging, and characteristic specification are straightforward.
- [Guide: Internationalization](https://lynxjs.org/guide/inclusion/internationalization): Enables localization for diverse users; core capabilities include using Intl API (with polyfills), i18next for simplicity, dynamic loading, wide support; i18next has an easy - to - use API.
- [Guide: Lynx DevTool](https://lynxjs.org/guide/debugging/lynx-devtool.md): Collection of performance and debugging tools for Lynx apps; enables device connection, selection, and debugging options; offers elements, console, sources, and layers panels for easy app debugging.
- [Lynx: Handle Errors](https://lynxjs.org/guide/debugging/handle-errors): Recognize errors in Lynx tools like bundle, JavaScript, and native module issues; use LogBox for quick error overview, handle errors in code; easily recognize errors with DevTool or LogBox.
- [Lynx: Layers Panel](https://lynxjs.org/guide/debugging/lynx-devtool/layers-panel): Helps understand Lynx page composition; enables viewing and inspecting page layers; easy access via command menu or more tools option.
- [Lynx DevTool: Console Panel](https://lynxjs.org/guide/debugging/lynx-devtool/console-panel): Use to view logged messages and run JavaScript; view JavaScript & client logs, filter messages, run code in REPL; simple to open and clear console.
- [Lynx DevTool: Elements Panel](https://lynxjs.org/guide/debugging/lynx-devtool/elements-panel): A powerful interface for inspecting and manipulating elements and element trees; allows CSS style viewing/modification, node inspection, and navigation; easy to open and use with intuitive controls.
- [Guide: Sources Panel](https://lynxjs.org/guide/debugging/lynx-devtool/sources-panel): Debug JavaScript in Lynx DevTool; has file navigation, code editing, and debugging sections; easy-to - use with clear options for opening, closing, and searching files, plus breakpoint management.
- [Guide: Custom Native Component in Web](https://lynxjs.org/guide/custom-native-component/custom-component-web): Customize web elements; directly refer to Web Components; straightforward approach by referring to official Web Components.
- [Guide: Implementing Custom Native Components on iOS](https://lynxjs.org/guide/custom-native-component/custom-component-iOS): Learn to implement custom native elements; capabilities include declaring/registering elements, creating native views, handling styles/properties, event binding; code examples simplify understanding.
- [Guide: Custom Native Element - Android Implementation](https://lynxjs.org/guide/custom-native-component/custom-component-android): Details custom native element implementation for Android; includes steps like element declaration/registration, view creation, style/property handling, event binding; code examples simplify understanding.
- [Lynx: Tutorial - Product Gallery](https://lynxjs.org/guide/start/tutorial-gallery): Build a product gallery page; learn to create components, add interactivity, use `<list>` for scrolling, and implement custom scrollbars; suitable for those new to Lynx and prefer hands - on learning.
- [Guide: Tutorial - Payment Details](https://lynxjs.org/guide/start/tutorial-payment-details): Details on payment - related tutorial; likely showcases steps or information for handling payment details; no ease - of - use statement available from given content.
- [Lynx: Integrating Lynx DevTool](https://lynxjs.org/guide/start/integrate-lynx-devtool.md): Guide for integrating Lynx DevTool; involves adding dependencies, registering service, and enabling switches on iOS & Android; clear steps for easy integration.
- [Lynx: Quick Start](https://lynxjs.org/guide/start/quick-start): Begin Lynx project development; covers system requirements, installation via `create-rspeedy`, Lynx Explorer setup; easy start with guided steps and QR - code - based app connection.
- [Lynx: Integrate with Existing Apps](https://lynxjs.org/guide/start/integrate-with-existing-apps): Integrate Lynx engine with native mobile or web apps; load Lynx apps via Lynx views; offers platform - specific integration steps for iOS, Android, and web.
- [Tutorial: Product Detail](https://lynxjs.org/guide/start/tutorial-product-detail): Implement a swiper component; learn direct node manipulation, main thread script use, and communication between main and background threads; simplifies code with hooks for better maintainability.
- [Lynx: Advanced DevTool Configurations](https://lynxjs.org/guide/start/integrate-lynx-devtool-advanced): Integrate DevTool switch page; Customize the page for diverse DevTool configurations; Code examples provided for iOS (Objective-C/Swift) and Android (Java/Kotlin).
- [Lynx: Integrate Lynx with Existing Web Apps](https://lynxjs.org/guide/start/fragments/web/integrating-lynx-with-web): Integrate Lynx apps into existing web projects; build web artifacts and integrate with new web projects; straightforward step - by - step process.
- [Lynx: Integrate Lynx with Existing Apps (iOS)](https://lynxjs.org/guide/start/fragments/ios/integrating-lynx-with-existing-app-ios.md): Integrate Lynx into iOS apps; configure dependencies with Cocoapods, initialize Lynx environment, render LynxView; easy steps with code examples for Obj-C and Swift.
- [Lynx: Integrate with Existing Android Apps](https://lynxjs.org/guide/start/fragments/android/integrating-lynx-with-existing-app-android.md): Guides on integrating Lynx into existing Android apps; involves dependency configuration, environment initialization, and LynxView rendering; provides clear steps and code examples for easy integration.
- [Guide: Custom Native Modules - Android Native Module](https://lynxjs.org/guide/custom-native-modules/native-module-android): Create custom native modules for Android in Lynx Explorer; involves creating module files in Java or Kotlin, registering with Lynx runtime; step - by - step guide for easy implementation.
- [Guide: Custom Native Modules - iOS Native Module](https://lynxjs.org/guide/custom-native-modules/native-module-ios): Steps to create iOS native modules; includes preparing Xcode project, implementing module in Objective - C or Swift, registering module; clear step - by - step instructions for building and running.
- [Guide: Embedding Lynx in Native iOS (Fixed Size)](https://lynxjs.org/guide/embed-lynx-to-native/embed-lynx-to-native-fixed-iOS): Set fixed LynxView size in iOS; can use `LynxViewBuilder#frame` or `preferredLayoutWidth/Height` with `layoutWidth/HeightMode`; straightforward iOS - Lynx integration.
- [Guide: Embed Lynx to Native - Fixed Android](https://lynxjs.org/guide/embed-lynx-to-native/embed-lynx-to-native-fixed-android): Set fixed size for LynxView on Android; when outer container constraint is `EXACTLY`, LynxView size is fixed; easily set size via code examples.
- [Guide: Embedding Lynx in Native - iOS (Flexible Sizing)](https://lynxjs.org/guide/embed-lynx-to-native/embed-lynx-to-native-flex-iOS): Control LynxView size based on content; avoid setting frame or set layout width/height mode to undefined or max; easy configuration via code snippets.
- [Guide: Embed Lynx to Native (Flex Android)](https://lynxjs.org/guide/embed-lynx-to-native/embed-lynx-to-native-flex-android): Layout constraints match Android views; LynxView size can adapt to content using WRAP_CONTENT; straightforward layout parameter setting in Java.
- [Lynx: Embedding Lynx in Android Native - Size Constraints and Layout](https://lynxjs.org/guide/embed-lynx-to-native/embed-lynx-to-native-mode-android): Get size constraints in `onMeasure` method; preset width and height to trigger layout early; easy to manage LynxView size in Android native.
- [Guide: Embedding Lynx in Native iOS](https://lynxjs.org/guide/embed-lynx-to-native/embed-lynx-to-native-mode-iOS): Set size constraints for LynxView on iOS; supports using methods like `updateViewportWithPreferredLayoutWidth` and `lynxviewbuilder#frame`; two constraint - setting ways and three supported constraint modes.
- [Lynx: Motion](https://lynxjs.org/guide/styling/animation): Offers extensive motion capabilities; can add transitions for layout/style changes, achieve stunning effects with keyframes; transitions are easy to use and automatically triggered.
- [Lynx: Typography](https://lynxjs.org/guide/styling/text-and-typography): Manage text in Lynx; use `<text>` element, add styles, mix text with images/views; easily control text display with CSS properties.
- [Guide: Visual Appearance](https://lynxjs.org/guide/styling/appearance): Manipulate background, borders, colors, gradients, clipping, and masking; use CSS properties like `background-image`, `border-radius` for creative effects; easy use of color values and gradient definitions.
- [Lynx: Theming](https://lynxjs.org/guide/styling/custom-theming): Offers theme customization; supports CSS descendant selectors, CSS variables for theme switching; easy to define and apply different theme styles.
- [Lynx: Evaluating Performance](https://lynxjs.org/guide/performance/evaluating-performance): Measures Lynx page performance; assesses user - perceived (UPP) and runtime (ARP) performance, offers built - in and custom metrics; helps optimize page load, rendering, and interactivity.
- [Lynx: Marking Lynx Pipeline](https://lynxjs.org/guide/performance/timing-flag): Monitor Lynx Pipeline performance for key components; set `__lynx_timing_flag` to mark pipelines, generate `PipelineEntry` events; simple process of marking nodes and getting performance data.
- [Guide: Interaction - Storage](https://lynxjs.org/guide/interaction/storage): No main title, feature description, core capabilities or ease - of - use statement found as content is empty.
- [Lynx: Visibility detection](https://lynxjs.org/guide/interaction/visibility-detection): Capabilities for detecting node visibility; includes unique exposure for monitoring node exposure, and an intersection observer for monitoring node intersections; easy to use for quick exposure or intersection detection.
- [Guide: Networking](https://lynxjs.org/guide/interaction/networking): Enables mobile apps to load remote resources; provides Fetch API like web for requests and response handling; easy to use with familiar async/await syntax.
- [Lynx: Event Handling](https://lynxjs.org/guide/interaction/event-handling): Web - like event mechanism for custom interaction logic; supports dual - threaded event processing for performance optimization; easy to listen and handle user click events with clear syntax.
- [Lynx: Instant First-Frame Rendering (IFR)](https://lynxjs.org/guide/interaction/ifr): Enables direct page content display on load; achieved via dual - thread architecture; can avoid white screen or intermediate states for better user experience.
- [Lynx: Event Propagation](https://lynxjs.org/guide/interaction/event-handling/event-propagation): Defines how events propagate; includes capture, bubble stages; allows event interception, supports cross - component event listening; easy to manage event handling with various event handler properties.
- [Guide: Direct Manipulation of Elements in React](https://lynxjs.org/guide/interaction/event-handling/manipulating-element.react): Enables direct element manipulation in React; core capabilities include using element references, selectors, and event target references; easy access to element manipulation APIs in both main and background threads.
- [Guide: Intersection Observer](https://lynxjs.org/guide/interaction/visibility-detection/intersection-observer): Observe intersection status of target & reference/ancestor nodes; Steps: create observer, specify reference, observe target; Easy to use with clear method steps.
- [Lynx: Exposure Ability](https://lynxjs.org/guide/interaction/visibility-detection/exposure-ability): Observe target node visibility changes; trigger exposure/anti - exposure events; useful for point reporting, UI lazy loading; simple event subscription and node - specific handling.


## api

- [API: API Reference](https://lynxjs.org/api/index): API reference overview; provides details about the API; no specific ease - of - use statement available in provided content.
- [API: CSS Properties](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties): No feature description available as content is empty; unable to identify core capabilities; no ease - of - use statement available as content is empty.
- [API: CSS Data Type](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/data-type): Defines values for CSS properties and functions; Core to CSS property value specification; Fundamental for understanding CSS value usage.
- [API: CSS Selectors](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/selectors): Define which elements a CSS rule applies to; covers selectors (type, class, ID, universal), combinators (descendant, child, sibling), pseudo-classes (`:active`, `:not()`, `:root`); easy to specify element - state - based selections.
- [Lynx CSS: At - rule](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/at-rule): Instructs CSS behavior; begins with @, followed by identifier; Lynx CSS supports various @rules with different syntaxes; clear and specific rule - based instruction.
- [CSS: `<percentage>`](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/data-type/percentage): Represents percentage values; used to define sizes relative to parent objects for properties like width, height, margin, etc.; easy to use with no space between number and symbol.
- [CSS Data Type: `<string>`](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/data-type/string): Represents sequence of characters in CSS; composed of Unicode characters in quotes, supports literal & hexadecimal code point representation; easy to understand and use for basic text representation in CSS.
- [CSS: `<color>` data type](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/data-type/color): Represents colors with optional alpha - transparency; definable via keywords, RGB (hexadecimal, `rgb()`, `rgba()`), and HSL (`hsl()`, `hsla()`); simple syntax for each definition method.
- [CSS Data Type: `<fit-content>`](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/data-type/fit-content): Defines a flexible sizing function; supports `width`, `height`, and grid layout properties; simple to use with straightforward sizing logic.
- [CSS Data Type: `<time>`](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/data-type/time): Represents time in seconds or milliseconds; used in animation, transition properties; easy to use with simple syntax of number followed by `s` or `ms` unit.
- [API: `<length-percentage>`](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/data-type/length-percentage): Represents length or percentage values; supports `calc()` for arithmetic operations; simple syntax for numerical value handling.
- [CSS Data Type: `<max-content>`](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/data-type/max-content): Defines size based on content; supports `width`, `height`, and grid layout properties; straightforward for sizing elements by content.
- [CSS: `<gradient>`](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/data-type/gradient): Represents a progressive color transition; Core capabilities include linear and radial gradients with custom angles, positions, and color - stops; Easy to define using specific functions like `linear - gradient()` and `radial - gradient()`.
- [CSS: `<angle>` Data Type](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/data-type/angle): Represents angle values in deg, rad, or turn units; used in gradients and transform functions; simple syntax with number and unit, and basic compatibility details provided.
- [CSS: `<length>`](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/data-type/length): Represents distance value; consists of number + unit (unit optional if number is 0), includes relative (rpx, rem, em, vh, vw) & absolute (px, ppx) units; syntax is straightforward with no space between number and unit.
- [CSS: `<number>`](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/data-type/number): Represents integers or numbers with fractional components; shows valid and invalid number examples; easily distinguishable valid/invalid numbers for CSS use.
- [API: animation - iteration - count](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/animation-iteration-count): Defines how many times an animation plays; supports 'infinite' and numeric values; simple property with straightforward syntax.
- [CSS: overflow - x](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/overflow-x): Specifies how to handle content overflow at left/right edges; has values like `visible` (default) and `hidden`; simple syntax with clear value descriptions.
- [API: CSS Custom properties (`--*`): CSS variables](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/css-variable): Define CSS variables with specific syntax; reference values using `var()`; use in declarations, with default values, in `calc` expressions; modify via JavaScript API or selector changes; easy for reusability and maintainability.
- [API: CSS min - height](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/min-height): Specify min height of a view; prevents `height` from exceeding `min - height`; priority: `max - height` > `min - height` > `height`. Easy to use with simple length or percentage syntax.
- [CSS: width](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/width): Specifies element width; values include `auto`, `<length>`, `<percentage>`, `max-content`, `fit-content`; default is border area width, different from web's content area width.
- [API: CSS clip - path](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/clip-path): Defines a clipping region for <view> elements; supports inset, circle, ellipse, path shapes; syntax is clear with well - defined values.
- [CSS: transform](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/transform): Rotate, scale, skew, or translate an element; supports various function values like `rotate`, `translate`, `scale`; easy to use with well - defined syntax.
- [CSS: grid - template - columns](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/grid-template-columns): Defines track sizing functions for grid columns; supports values like `<length>`, `<percentage>`, `<flex>`; easy to set column widths and track sizing with various syntax options.
- [API: CSS z - index](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/z-index): Sets z - order of elements and descendants; larger z - index covers smaller ones; creates stacking context with `z-index: 0`; simple integer - based syntax.
- [CSS: opacity](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/opacity): Sets element opacity; controls degree of transparency; applies to element and contents; be cautious of group opacity differences on Android and iOS; simple numeric syntax.
- [API: CSS transform - origin](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/transform-origin): Sets origin for element transformations; accepts one or two values for x - and y - offsets in length, percentage, or keyword form; simple syntax for defining transform origins.
- [CSS: padding - right](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/padding-right): Sets right - side padding width of an element; supports `<length>` and `<percentage>` values, with `0px` as default; simple syntax for specifying padding.
- [API: -x-auto-font-size](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/-x-auto-font-size): Sets text adaptive font size; controls enable/disable, min/max font size, and adjustment granularity; simple syntax with optional parameters.
- [CSS: border - left - color](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-left-color): Sets the color of the left border; accepts values like transparent, specific colors (hex, RGB, HSLA); simple one - property syntax for border - left color styling.
- [API: CSS max - width](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/max-width): Specify max width of a view; prevents `width` from exceeding set value, has higher priority than `width` and `min-width`; simple syntax with `<length>` and `<percentage>` values.
- [CSS: position](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/position): Determines element positioning in a document; supports relative, absolute, fixed, and sticky positioning; easy to use with simple syntax for each positioning type.
- [API: relative - align - inline - end](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/relative-align-inline-end): Specifies element alignment relative to parent/sibling; has different equivalents based on direction (ltr/rtl); simple syntax with values like "parent", "none", or numbers.
- [API: CSS filter Property](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/filter): Applies graphical effects like blur or color shift; currently supports `grayscale` and `blur` functions; offers performance - related tips for Android.
- [CSS: margin - inline - start](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/margin-inline-start): Defines logical inline start margin; corresponds to margin - left in LTR, margin - right in RTL; supports length, percentage, auto values; Lynx has differences like non - collapsing margins and specific 'auto' support.
- [API: animation - delay](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/animation-delay): Defines delay before animation starts; has syntax allowing single or multiple time values; Lynx requires units for duration and delay for proper behavior.
- [CSS: border - inline - start - width](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-inline-start-width): Defines width of logical inline - start border; corresponds to border - left/right - width based on writing mode and direction; simple syntax with values like thin, medium, thick, and <length>.
- [API: CSS Visibility](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/visibility): Controls element visibility without changing layout; supports `visible` and `hidden` values; simple syntax for showing or hiding elements.
- [API: CSS background - color](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/background-color): Sets element's background color; supports color values like hexadecimal, RGB, HSLA; easy to use with simple syntax.
- [API: CSS flex - basis](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/flex-basis): Sets initial main size of a flex item; overrides width/height when set; has values like auto, length, percentage; simple syntax for straightforward sizing.
- [CSS: text - shadow](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/text-shadow): Adds shadows to text; has specific syntax for offset, blur - radius, and color; only supports one shadow layer, and all values are mandatory with color last.
- [API: -x-handle-color](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/-x-handle-color): Specifies floating marker color in text copy selection; valid only in selection pseudo - element; value is `<color>` type.
- [API: CSS padding - top](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/padding-top): Sets top - padding height of an element; supports <length>, <percentage> values; default value is 0px, negative values not allowed.
- [API: animation - name](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/animation-name): Specifies animations for an element; names reference @keyframes sequences; simple syntax for single or multiple animations.
- [CSS: border - left - style](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-left-style): Sets left border line style; supports values like none, hidden, dotted, dashed, solid; simple syntax for specifying border appearance.
- [CSS: border - radius](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-radius): Rounds an element's outer border edge; can create circular or elliptical corners; specified with one - four length or percentage values, and optionally an additional set for ellipses; simple syntax for corner - rounding.
- [CSS: letter - spacing](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/letter-spacing): Sets horizontal text character spacing; supports only `<length>` values for extra inter - character space; simple syntax with `<length>` specification.
- [API: relative - align - bottom](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/relative-align-bottom): In relative - layout, aligns current element's bottom edge; values include 'none', 'parent', and <number> for sibling alignment; straightforward syntax and value definition.
- [API: border - bottom - left - radius](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-bottom-left-radius): Adds rounded border to bottom - left corner; sets horizontal and vertical radii, with various value types like length and percentage; easy to understand syntax for corner - rounding.
- [CSS: flex - shrink](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/flex-shrink): Defines flex element's shrink factor on main axis; scales elements to prevent container overflow; simple syntax with <number> values (default 1), no negative values.
- [API: CSS border - right - width](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-right-width): Sets the width of the right border of an element; supports keyword values (thin, medium, thick) and `<length>` values; has a formal definition and syntax.
- [API: column - gap (grid - column - gap)](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/column-gap): Sets gap between columns in flex & grid layouts; accepts <length>, <percentage>, calc() values; syntax is straightforward with common CSS value types.
- [API: Deprecated linear - weight - sum](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/linear-weight-sum): Determines child element weight sum in linear layout; calculates width allocation based on linear - weight values; requires fixed main axis size for linear container, can't use fixed value and weight simultaneously.
- [API: linear - cross - gravity](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/linear-cross-gravity): Deprecated API; sets child element position on cross axis in linear layout; values include none, start, end, center, stretch; no Web equivalent.
- [API: animation - direction](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/animation-direction): Specifies animation play direction; values include normal, reverse, alternate, alternate - reverse; simple property syntax and clear value explanations.
- [CSS: text - overflow](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/text-overflow): Sets how hidden overflow text is signaled to users; supports 'clip' to truncate text, 'ellipsis' to show ellipsis; straightforward syntax with clear values.
- [API: border - bottom - width](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-bottom-width): Sets bottom border width; supports values like thin, medium, thick, and <length>; simple syntax with clear value definitions.
- [API: CSS animation property](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/animation): A shorthand for multiple animation - related properties; can specify keyframe name, duration, timing function, etc.; syntax is flexible for single or multiple animations.
- [CSS: border - end - end - radius](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-end-end-radius): Defines logical border radius; corresponds to physical border radius based on writing mode & direction; Syntax includes circle/ellipsis values like <length> or <percentage>.
- [API: CSS align - self](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/align-self): Controls element alignment on cross or block axis in flex, linear, and grid layouts; overrides `align - items`; has values like `auto`, `stretch`, `center`; easy to understand with clear syntax examples.
- [API: CSS aspect - ratio](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/aspect-ratio): Sets preferred aspect - ratio for boxes; used in auto size & layout calculations; syntax includes ratios like 1/1, 16/9; simple to define ratios for layout control.
- [API: CSS overflow - y](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/overflow-y): Specifies top and bottom edge clipping of overflow content; has values like visible (default) and hidden; simple syntax with clear value descriptions.
- [CSS: margin - left](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/margin-left): Sets left - hand margin of an element; accepts length, percentage, `auto` values; Lynx has non - collapsing margins and specific `auto` support in flex, grid, linear layouts.
- [API: CSS animation - duration](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/animation-duration): Defines animation cycle length; examples and syntax provided; requires units for duration to avoid undefined behavior.
- [API: CSS image - rendering](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/image-rendering): Sets image scaling algorithm for `<image>` and `background - image`; supports 'auto', 'crisp - edges', 'pixelated' values; simple syntax for image resizing.
- [API: relative-inline-end-of](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/relative-inline-end-of): Defines element's position relative to sibling; Equivalent to relative-right-of or relative-left-of based on direction; Simple CSS - like syntax for easy use.
- [CSS: margin - top](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/margin-top): Sets top - margin of an element; positive value moves it away, negative moves it closer; supports length, percentage, auto values; Lynx has no margin collapse, has specific `auto` support.
- [API: CSS align - content](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/align-content): Defines flex lines' cross - axis alignment in flex layout, block - axis alignment in grid layout; has values like stretch, start, end; syntax is well - defined with specific values.
- [API: CSS border Property](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border): Sets an element's border; shorthand for border - width, border - style, border - color; has simple syntax for width, style, color values; Lynx has some differences from MDN.
- [CSS: border - inline - end - style](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-inline-end-style): Defines logical inline end border style; has values like none, dotted, solid; corresponds to border - right/left - style based on writing mode and direction.
- [CSS: align - items](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/align-items): Sets align - self on direct children; controls cross - axis alignment in flex/linear, block - axis in grid; values include stretch, center, start, end, etc.; easy to understand syntax for layout alignment.
- [API: CSS justify - self](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/justify-self): Sets box justification in alignment container along axis; aligns items in grid areas on inline axis; values include auto, stretch, center, start, end, with simple syntax.
- [CSS: padding](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/padding): Sets all - four - side padding at once; shorthand for padding - top, - right, - bottom, - left; simple syntax with 1 - 4 values of length or percentage.
- [CSS: grid - row - end](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/grid-row-end): Specifies grid item's end position in grid row; supports values like `auto`, `<number>`, `span && <number>`; clear syntax and value explanations.
- [CSS: text - indent](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/text-indent): Defines indentation of first line of text content; supports <length> and <percentage> values; syntax is straightforward, but note difference in percentage application compared to web.
- [CSS: line - height](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/line-height): Sets line box height for text; values include normal, number, length; only affects <text> element; best to set value greater than font - size for non - clipping.
- [API: CSS border - width](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-width): Shorthand for setting all four border widths; supports 1 - 4 value syntax and values like thin, medium, thick, and lengths; simple syntax for border width customization.
- [API: CSS text - stroke - width](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/text-stroke-width): Specifies text stroke width; supports length values like px, em, pt; simple syntax for setting width.
- [API: relative - align - left](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/relative-align-left): In relative - layout, aligns current element's left edge; values include `none`, `parent`, and `<number>` for parent or sibling alignment; simple syntax for layout control.
- [CSS: background - clip](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/background-clip): Defines background clipping area; supports values border - box, padding - box, content - box; simple syntax for setting background clipping.
- [API: row - gap (grid - row - gap)](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/row-gap): Sets row gap in flex & grid layouts; supports `<length>`, `<percentage>`, and `calc()` values; simple syntax for specifying row - to - row spacing.
- [API: CSS padding - bottom](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/padding-bottom): Sets bottom padding height; uses length or percentage values; negative values not allowed; syntax is straightforward with default value of 0px.
- [API: -x-handle-size](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/-x-handle-size): Specifies floating marker size in text selection; only valid in selection pseudo - element; value is a `<length>`; Lynx - specific property.
- [CSS: grid - template - rows](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/grid-template-rows): Defines track sizing functions for grid rows; supports <length>, <percentage>, <flex> and other values; has a clear and straightforward syntax for specifying row sizes.
- [CSS: justify - content](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/justify-content): Defines space distribution along main - axis in flex, linear, grid layouts; has values like center, start, space - between; simple syntax with well - defined values and clear behavior.
- [API: animation - play - state](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/animation-play-state): Specifies animation's running or paused state; resumes from pause point; simple CSS - based syntax for single or multiple animations.
- [API: CSS border-inline-end-color](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-inline-end-color): Defines logical inline - end border color; corresponds to border - right/left - color based on writing mode and direction; simple color - setting syntax with values like transparent and <color>.
- [CSS: border - top - width](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-top-width): Sets the width of the top border of an element; supports keyword values (thin, medium, thick) and <length> values; has a straightforward syntax for specifying border width.
- [API: relative - center](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/relative-center): Sets centering alignment in relative - layout; values include `none`, `both`, `vertical`, `horizontal`; straightforward syntax for easy centering setup.
- [CSS: text - decoration](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/text-decoration): Sets decorative lines on text; supports values for line type (underline, line - through, none), color, and style (solid, double, dotted etc.); simple syntax for common text decoration needs.
- [CSS: flex - wrap](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/flex-wrap): Determines if flex items stay on one line or wrap; values include nowrap, wrap, wrap - reverse; simple syntax for controlling flex item layout.
- [CSS: font - family](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/font-family): Specifies prioritized font family list for an element; Syntax is `font-family: font1,font2`; easy to define font choices for elements.
- [CSS: padding - inline - end](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/padding-inline-end): Defines logical inline end padding; maps to physical padding based on `direction`; values include `0px`, `<length>`, `<percentage>`; simple syntax for setting padding.
- [API: CSS min - width](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/min-width): Specify minimum view width; prevents `width` from exceeding `min-width` value; simple syntax with `<length>` and `<percentage>` values.
- [API: grid - column - span](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/grid-column-span): Deprecated API; contributes grid span to item placement; overridden by `grid-column-start` and `grid-column-end`; simple <number> - based syntax.
- [CSS: grid - column - end](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/grid-column-end): Defines a grid item's end position in grid column; supports <number>, span <number>, and auto values; syntax is straightforward with clear value explanations.
- [CSS: gap](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/gap): Sets gaps between rows and columns in flex and grid layouts; shorthand for `row - gap` and `column - gap`; specified as `<length>` or `<percentage>` values.
- [CSS: transition - timing - function](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/transition-timing-function): Sets how intermediate values are calculated for transition - affected CSS properties; supports values like linear, ease, cubic - bezier(); clearly defined syntax and formal definition.
- [API: CSS vertical - align Property](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/vertical-align): Sets vertical alignment of inline elements; supports <length>, <percent>, baseline, middle, center, top, bottom, text - top, text - bottom, super, sub; easy to understand and set values.
- [CSS: flex](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/flex): Sets how a flex item grows or shrinks in a flex container; shorthand for flex - grow, flex - shrink, and flex - basis; has one, two, or three - value syntax with clear value interpretations.
- [API: CSS grid - auto - rows](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/grid-auto-rows): Defines size of implicitly - created grid row tracks; supports keyword, length, percentage, flex, minmax(), and fit - content values; clear syntax and formal definition for easy understanding.
- [API: CSS white - space](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/white-space): Sets how white space inside an element is handled; supports 'normal' and 'nowrap' values; simple syntax for controlling text wrapping.
- [API: animation - timing - function](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/animation-timing-function): Specifies animation progress through cycles; accepts keyword values (e.g., ease, linear), function values (cubic - bezier, steps); has straightforward syntax for single or multiple animations.
- [CSS: grid - column - start](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/grid-column-start): Defines grid item's start position in grid column; can use values like `auto`, `<number>`, `span && <number>`; syntax is well - defined and easy to understand.
- [API: CSS linear - gravity](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/linear-gravity): Deprecated CSS property; defines space distribution in linear containers, similar to `justify - content`; easy to use with simple syntax, but some values are deprecated.
- [CSS: flex - direction](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/flex-direction): Defines main axis and direction of flex items; has values like row, row - reverse, column, column - reverse; simple syntax for layout control.
- [API: CSS Font Style](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/font-style): Sets the font style; supports values like normal, italic, oblique; straightforward syntax for easy font style specification.
- [API: border - left - width](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-left-width): Sets left border width; supports values like thin, medium, thick, and <length>; simple syntax for border width specification.
- [CSS: perspective](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/perspective): Determines distance between z=0 plane and user for 3D - positioned elements; has 'auto' and '<length>' values; syntax is clear and straightforward.
- [CSS: border - right](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-right): Sets all right - border properties of an element; shorthand for width, style, color; easy to define border properties with simple syntax.
- [API: box - sizing](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/box-sizing): Defines how fixed sizes are assigned; has values `border - box` and `content - box` affecting size calculation; simple syntax for setting box sizing.
- [CSS: left](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/left): Specifies horizontal position of a positioned element; depends on `position` value, can be `auto`, `<length>`, `<percentage>`; simple syntax for positioning control.
- [CSS: direction](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/direction): Sets text direction; values include normal, ltr, rtl, and lynx - rtl (deprecated); simple syntax for specifying text flow in different languages.
- [CSS: border - inline - start - style](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-inline-start-style): Defines logical inline start border style; corresponds to `border - left - style` in LTR or `border - right - style` in RTL; syntax includes values like `none`, `solid`, etc.
- [CSS: color](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/color): Sets text foreground color; supports named - colors, hex - colors, rgb() and hsl() values; simple syntax for specifying color values.
- [CSS: margin - right](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/margin-right): Sets right - hand margin of an element; accepts length, percentage, auto values; easy to understand with clear syntax examples and compatibility details.
- [API: CSS font - weight](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/font-weight): Sets font weight; supports keyword values (normal, bold) and numeric values (100 - 1000); simple property to adjust text boldness.
- [CSS: height](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/height): Specifies element height; values include `auto`, `<length>`, `<percentage>`, `max-content`, `fit-content`; syntax is straightforward with clear value definitions.
- [API: relative - right - of](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/relative-right-of): Positions current element to right of specified sibling; values include `none` (default) and `<number>` (sibling element number); simple syntax, no web equivalent.
- [CSS: background - repeat](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/background-repeat): Sets how background images are repeated; supports values like repeat, no - repeat, repeat - x, repeat - y, space, round; easy to define repetition along axes or control non - repetition.
- [CSS: border - top - right - radius](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-top-right-radius): Rounds the bottom - left corner of an element; specifies circle or ellipse radius with length or percentage values; straightforward syntax for corner - rounding.
- [CSS: transition - duration](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/transition-duration): Sets length of transition animation completion time; uses `<time>` values like `2s` or `300ms`; easy to define animation durations with simple syntax.
- [API: linear - layout - gravity](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/linear-layout-gravity): Deprecated API for child element position in linear container; sets position perpendicular to layout direction; syntax includes values like none, stretch, start, end, center; easy to understand with examples and syntax details.
- [API: inset - inline - start](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/inset-inline-start): Defines logical inline start inset; maps to physical offset based on directionality; has same syntax and values as `left` property; easy to understand for those familiar with CSS offset properties.
- [API: relative-inline-start-of](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/relative-inline-start-of): Positions element relative to sibling by ID in relative layout; equivalent to relative-left-of or relative-right-of based on direction; simple syntax for specifying values.
- [API: relative - top - of](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/relative-top-of): Positions current element relative to top of sibling; values include `none` (default) and `<number>` (sibling number); simple syntax for layout control.
- [CSS: border - inline - start - color](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-inline-start-color): Defines logical inline start border color; has values like transparent, <color>; simple syntax for setting border color.
- [API: border - bottom - right - radius](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-bottom-right-radius): Adds rounded border to bottom - right corner; sets horizontal and vertical radii with length or percentage values; simple syntax for corner - rounding.
- [API: CSS text - align](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/text-align): Sets horizontal alignment of inline - level content in block elements; supports values like left, right, center, start, end; syntax is straightforward with simple keyword values.
- [API: CSS padding - left](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/padding-left): Sets left - side padding width of an element; supports length and percentage values; has a clear and straightforward syntax.
- [API: border - bottom - color](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-bottom-color): Sets bottom border color of an element; uses <color> values like rgb, rgba, hex - color; simple syntax for specifying bottom border color separately.
- [API: -x-auto-font-size-preset-sizes](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/-x-auto-font-size-preset-sizes): Specifies array of preset font sizes for auto - scaling; largest suitable size picked within width constraints; only effective when `-x-auto-font-size` is enabled.
- [API: CSS - mask](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/mask): Hides elements via masking or clipping; shorthand for mask - clip, - image, - origin, - position, - repeat, - size; has a well - defined syntax with multiple value types.
- [CSS: border - top](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-top): Sets all right - border properties; shorthand for border - top - width, style, color; simple syntax, with default style as "solid".
- [API: relative - align - inline - start](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/relative-align-inline-start): Specifies element alignment relative to parent/sibling; has different equivalents based on direction (ltr/rtl); simple syntax with values like 'parent', 'none', or numbers.
- [API: border - start - start - radius](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-start-start-radius): Defines logical border radius; corresponds to top - left or top - right in different writing modes; simple syntax with length or percentage values.
- [API: CSS linear - direction](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/linear-direction): Sets linear item placement in a container; defines main axis and direction with values like column, row, and their reversed counterparts; simple syntax for specifying layout directions.
- [CSS: border - right - color](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-right-color): Sets right border color; accepts values like transparent, <color> (e.g., red, #ffbb00, rgb, hsla, currentColor); simple single - property syntax.
- [API: box - shadow](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/box-shadow): Adds shadow effect to element frames; sets X - Y offsets, blur, diffusion, and color values; simple syntax for specifying shadow properties.
- [API: border - bottom - style](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-bottom-style): Sets bottom border style of an element; supports values like none, hidden, dotted, dashed, etc.; easy to specify separate bottom border style.
- [CSS: background - position](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/background-position): Sets initial background image position relative to background - origin; supports keyword, percentage, length values in 1 - or 2 - value syntax; no global values or edge offsets.
- [API: CSS mask - image](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/mask-image): Sets an element's mask - image; supports `none` keyword and `<image>` values like gradients and URLs; lacks support for SVG, `image()` function, global values.
- [CSS: transition - delay](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/transition-delay): Specifies the wait duration before starting a property's transition effect; values are of `<time>` type with a default of 0s; syntax is straightforward, requires units for duration and delay.
- [CSS: justify - items](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/justify-items): Defines default justify - self for box items; aligns items in grid areas on inline axis; supports values like stretch, center, start, end; simple syntax for grid container alignment.
- [CSS: border - right - style](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-right-style): Sets right border line style of an element; supports values like none, hidden, dotted, dashed, etc.; straightforward syntax for defining border styles.
- [CSS: text - stroke](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/text-stroke): Defines text character stroke width and color; supports length for width, color for stroke color; simple CSS - like syntax for easy text stroke styling.
- [CSS: grid - auto - flow](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/grid-auto-flow): Controls auto - placement algorithm in CSS grids; supports values like row, column, dense, row dense, column dense; simple syntax for specifying item flow in grids.
- [CSS: border - color](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-color): Sets border colors of an element; supports specifying 1 - 4 <color> values for different border sides; simple syntax for defining border colors.
- [API: CSS text - stroke - color](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/text-stroke-color): Defines text stroke color; supports `<color>` values like named colors, hex, RGB; simple one - value syntax for specifying color.
- [API: CSS relative - id](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/relative-id): Sets number of sibling elements in relative - layout; has special values `-1` (default, not alignment ref) and `0` (not allowed); simple syntax like `relative - id: 1`; no web equivalent.
- [CSS: right](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/right): Specifies horizontal position of a positioned element; depends on `position` value; for `absolute`/`fixed` it's distance to containing block's right, for `relative` it's leftward shift; simple syntax with length, percentage, or `auto` values.
- [CSS: border - top - style](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-top-style): Sets the line style of an element's top border; supports values like none, hidden, dotted, dashed, solid, etc.; has a simple, CSS - like syntax for easy declaration.
- [CSS: grid - auto - columns](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/grid-auto-columns): Defines sizes for implicitly - created grid column tracks; supports values like auto, length, percentage, flex, max - content, minmax, fit - content; clear syntax and value descriptions.
- [API: CSS font - size](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/font-size): Sets font size; affects relative length units like em and ex; supports `<length>` and `<percentage>` values; has simple syntax.
- [CSS: inset - inline - end](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/inset-inline-end): Defines logical inline end inset; maps to right/left based on directionality; syntax includes length, percentage, auto, and calc values; easy to understand with values like left property.
- [API: grid - row - span](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/grid-row-span): Deprecated API; contributes grid span to item placement; overridden by grid - row - start/end; simple number - based syntax.
- [CSS: background](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/background): Shorthand property for setting all background styles; sets properties like color, image, position, size, and repeat; easy to use with a straightforward syntax.
- [API: CSS flex - grow](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/flex-grow): Defines flex grow factor for flex items; allocates remaining space in flex container based on factor values; simple number - based syntax, defaults to 0.
- [CSS: border - style](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-style): Sets styles of all four borders; supports 1 - 4 value syntax with various style values; has differences from web (default value, border width with none/hidden, no global values).
- [API: CSS animation-fill-mode](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/animation-fill-mode): Sets how CSS animation applies styles before and after execution; supports values like none, forwards, backwards, both; simple syntax for single or multiple animations.
- [CSS: border - start - end - radius](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-start-end-radius): Defines logical border radius; corresponds to physical border-radius props based on writing mode; simple syntax with <length> or <percentage> values.
- [Lynx CSS: display](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/display): Determines internal element layout; supports values like flex, linear, none, grid, relative; easy to understand with clear syntax and examples.
- [CSS: background - origin](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/background-origin): Sets background origin; values include border-box, padding-box, content-box; simple syntax for specifying origin relative to border, padding, or content box.
- [CSS: margin](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/margin): Sets margin area on all four sides of an element; shorthand for margin - top, -right, -bottom, -left; has syntax for one - four values, various value types. Easy to use with different value specifications.
- [CSS: border - top - color](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-top-color): Sets top border color; supports values like transparent, <color> (e.g., red, #ffbb00, rgb/hsla values, currentColor); simple syntax for color specification.
- [API: relative - left - of](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/relative-left-of): Positions current element left of specified sibling; values include `none` (default) and `<number>` (sibling number); simple syntax, no web equivalent.
- [API: CSS top Property](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/top): Specifies vertical position in CSS; effect depends on `position` value (absolute/fixed/relative); easy to understand syntax with length, percentage, and `auto` values.
- [CSS Property: background - image](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/background-image): Sets one or more background images on an element; supports image formats like jpg, png; has bindbgload and bindbgerror events; easy to understand syntax.
- [CSS: border - inline - end - width](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-inline-end-width): Defines width of logical inline - end border; corresponds to border - right/left - width based on writing mode and direction; simple syntax with values like thin, medium, thick, <length>.
- [API: linear - weight](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/linear-weight): Defines child element's weight in linear layout; parent allocates space based on weight ratio; simple syntax with `<number>` values, default 0, no negative values.
- [API: relative - bottom - of](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/relative-bottom-of): Positions current element below a specified sibling; Values are `none` (default) or `<number>` (int for sibling number); Simple syntax for relative layout positioning.
- [API: CSS overflow Property](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/overflow): Specifies content clipping when element content is large; has `visible` and `hidden` values; simple syntax for controlling content visibility.
- [CSS: border - left](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-left): Shorthand for setting left border styles; includes width, style, color; simple syntax, e.g., `border - left: 1px solid red;`.
- [CSS API: word - break](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/word-break): Specifies how to break lines within a word; has values like normal, break - all, keep - all; simple syntax for line - break rule specification.
- [CSS: background - size](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/background-size): Sets background image size; values include cover, contain, auto, length, percentage; easy to adjust image scaling in CSS for backgrounds.
- [CSS: border - top - left - radius](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-top-left-radius): Rounds the top - left corner of an element; uses circle or ellipse to define curvature; specifies radius with length or percentage values; syntax is straightforward for corner - rounding.
- [CSS: margin-inline-end](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/margin-inline-end): Defines logical inline end margin; has <length>, <percentage>, `0px`, `auto` values; easy to set margin based on layout direction with clear syntax.
- [API: CSS max - height](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/max-height): Specify max height of a view; prevents `height` from exceeding set value, has high priority; syntax includes `<length>` and `<percentage>` values.
- [CSS: bottom](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/bottom): Specifies vertical position; for `absolute`/`fixed` positions, distance from containing block's bottom; for `relative`, upward displacement from normal position; simple syntax with length, percentage, or `auto` values.
- [API: relative - align - right](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/relative-align-right): In relative - layout, aligns current element's right edge to parent or sibling; values include none, parent, <number>; simple and clear syntax for layout alignment.
- [CSS: flex - flow](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/flex-flow): Shorthand for flex - direction and flex - wrap; specifies flex container direction and wrapping behavior; simple syntax for common layout settings.
- [API: relative - align - top](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/relative-align-top): In relative - layout, aligns current element's top edge to parent or sibling; values include `none`, `parent`, `<number>`; simple syntax for layout alignment.
- [API: CSS border - bottom](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-bottom): Sets bottom border styles; allows setting width, style, color in order; simple syntax for quick border configuration.
- [CSS: order](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/order): Sets item layout order in container; items sorted by ascending order value then source code order; default value is 0; simple numerical - based syntax.
- [CSS: border - end - start - radius](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/border-end-start-radius): Defines logical border radius; corresponds to other border - radius props in different writing modes/directions; simple syntax with <length> or <percentage> values.
- [CSS: transition](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/transition): Shorthand for transition - related properties; sets property, duration, timing - function, and delay; simple syntax for single or multiple properties.
- [API: CSS padding-inline-start](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/padding-inline-start): Defines logical inline start padding; maps to physical padding based on `direction`; syntax includes `<length>` and `<percentage>` values; simple to use with clear syntax.
- [CSS: margin - bottom](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/margin-bottom): Sets bottom margin of an element; accepts length, percentage, `auto` values; has differences from web (no margin collapse, limited `auto` support) and Lynx - specific compatibility.
- [CSS: transition - property](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/transition-property): Sets CSS properties for transition effects; supports values like none, all, custom - idents; defines which properties (e.g., width, color) are affected by transitions; syntax is straightforward.
- [CSS: grid - row - start](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/properties/grid-row-start): Defines grid item's start position in grid row; can use number, span & number, or auto value; simple syntax for grid - based layout placement.
- [API: CSS @font-face](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/at-rule/font-face): Defines custom fonts; can load from remote servers or local user fonts; simple syntax for specifying font - family and src values.
- [CSS: @import](https://lynxjs.org/api/css/at-rule/import): Imports style rules from other stylesheets; has a simple syntax `@import <string>`; easy way to incorporate external style sheets.
- [Lynx: `<text>`](https://lynxjs.org/api/elements/built-in/text): Lynx's built - in text - display component; supports text styling, click events, and nests `<text>`, `<image>`, `<view>`; simple to use with clear layout and event - binding mechanisms.
- [API: `<view>` Element](https://lynxjs.org/api/elements/built-in/view): Basic element for containing others; has drawing & layout capabilities, many attributes for behavior/appearance, numerous events, and useful methods like `boundingClientRect`; straightforward usage with clear attribute and method descriptions.
- [API: `<scroll-view>`](https://lynxjs.org/api/elements/built-in/scroll-view): Basic scrolling component; supports horizontal and vertical scrolling, scroll events, and sticky capability; easy to configure with various attributes and methods.
- [API: `<image>` Element](https://lynxjs.org/api/elements/built-in/image): Displays web, static, and local images; supports cropping/scaling, styling, special effects, aspect ratio adaptation, and event binding; easy to use with clear property and event definitions.
- [API: `<list>` Element](https://lynxjs.org/api/elements/built-in/list): High - performance scrollable container; supports lazy loading, recycling; offers horizontal/vertical scroll, multi - column/layouts; easy to configure attributes, events, and methods for custom behavior.
- [API: `<page>` Element](https://lynxjs.org/api/elements/built-in/page): Serves as the root node per page; can be omitted with framework - generated root, or used explicitly for styling and event binding; easy to style using CSS selectors or manage with event bindings.
- [Lynx API: Lynx](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx): No title or content provided; unable to determine features, capabilities, or ease - of - use statement.
- [Lynx API: NodesRef](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/nodes-ref): Represents reference to UI nodes; core capabilities include querying attributes, executing UI methods, querying path info, setting native props; easy to use with examples provided for querying node position/size.
- [Lynx API: Main Thread APIs](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/main-thread): Enables JavaScript execution on main thread; used for smooth animations, gesture handling; resolves multi - thread response delay for near - native experience.
- [Lynx API: Global](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/global): No specific content provided; unable to determine core capabilities and ease - of - use statement.
- [Lynx: Performance APIs](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/performance-api): Tools for precisely measuring Lynx app performance; helps pinpoint bottlenecks in loading, rendering, user interactions; provides built - in metrics, with concepts like Entry Object, Performance Observer, and Events.
- [Lynx API: SelectorQuery](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/selector-query): Gets node references for method calls and property access; core methods include `exec`, `select`, `selectAll`, `selectRoot`, `selectUniqueID`; easy to create and use for node operations.
- [Lynx API: IntersectionObserver](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/intersection-observer): Observe intersection status between target & reference/ancestor nodes; core methods include relativeTo, relativeToViewport, observe, disconnect; easy 3 - 4 step process to monitor node exposure.
- [Lynx API: Lynx Request Animation Frame](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/main-thread/lynx-request-animation-frame): Likely related to handling animation frames; Core capabilities not clear from provided content; Ease - of - use statement not clear from provided content.
- [Lynx API: Lynx Global Props](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/main-thread/lynx-global-props): Allows use of global properties; Facilitates passing data across components; Seamless integration with Lynx API components.
- [Lynx API: Main Thread - Element](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/main-thread/main-thread-element): Represents an element in main - thread scripts; accesses/modifies element properties, with methods like get/set attribute, style, query selector; easy access to element - related operations.
- [Lynx: lynx.querySelectorAll()](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/main-thread/lynx-query-select-all): Find all elements matching a selector among page's child elements; has simple syntax with one parameter; easy to use for element selection.
- [Lynx: lynx.querySelector()](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/main-thread/lynx-query-selector): Finds first matching element in page's child elements; uses specified CSS selector; simple one - line syntax for element retrieval.
- [API: Lynx - Main Thread - Lynx Get Text Info](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/main-thread/lynx-get-text-info): No clear feature description in provided content; unable to identify core capabilities; no ease - of - use statement available.
- [Lynx: cancelAnimationFrame() static method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-cancel-animation-frame): Cancels a `requestAnimationFrame` callback; has a specific syntax with a `requestID` parameter; simple and straightforward to use for callback cancellation.
- [Lynx: requireModuleAsync() static method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-require-module-async): Imports modules asynchronously; allows other JS code execution during loading; requires specific module format, can use @lynx - js/runtime - wrapper - webpack - plugin; has error handling in callback.
- [Lynx: requestResourcePrefetch() static method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-request-resource-prefetch): Proactively preload CDN resources from frontend; supports image, video, audio types; simple API with defined parameters and callback for status handling.
- [Lynx: registerModule() static method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-register-module): Registers a JavaScript value for single LynxView use; takes name and module as parameters, returns undefined; easy to use with straightforward syntax for sharing values within a view.
- [Lynx API: getElementById()](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-get-element-by-id): Finds element reference by id in current page; takes id selector as param, returns NodeRef or null; simple syntax, clear reasons for potential node - selection failures.
- [Lynx API: requestAnimationFrame()](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-request-animation-frame): Invokes callback at next VSYNC for animation effects; takes callback with timestamp param, returns ID to cancel; simple syntax for animation handling.
- [Lynx: createIntersectionObserver() static method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-create-intersection-observer): Create an IntersectionObserver object; customize behavior via parameters like thresholds, initialRatio, observeAll; simple 4 - step process to observe intersection status changes.
- [Lynx: reportError() static method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-report-error): Manually report JavaScript errors; accepts string or Error object, with options for error level; simple syntax for error reporting in various scenarios like sync/async code.
- [Lynx: getJSModule() static method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-get-js-module): Acquire a single - LynxView - scoped JavaScript value; takes module name as param, returns registered value or `undefined` if not registered; simple syntax for module retrieval.
- [Lynx: Performance Readonly Object](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-performance): Offers access to page - related performance metrics; key capability is creating a `PerformanceObserver` to receive Lynx - generated performance events; simple method to monitor performance events.
- [Lynx: addFont() static method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-add-font): Dynamically adds fonts; takes fontFace and optional callback as parameters; simple method call for font addition.
- [Lynx: GlobalProps](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-global-props): Global read - only variable in front - end; client can set/update via Lynx SDK API, triggering full - page re - render; easy access and usage in front - end code with examples provided.
- [Lynx: setObserverFrameRate() static method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-set-observer-frame-rate): Sets exposure detection frequency; configures `forPageRect` and `forExposureCheck` intervals in fps; straightforward method with clear parameter interface.
- [Lynx API: animate()](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-animate-api): Shortcut for setting CSS Animation on UI elements in JS; supports two keyframe formats, various options; returns an `Animation` object with control methods, easy to use for basic UI animations.
- [Lynx: createSelectorQuery() static method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-create-selector-query): Create a `SelectorQuery` with page root; obtain node position and size; simple to use with a clear syntax.
- [Lynx API: BeforePublishEvent](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-before-publish-event): Manages element event listening; core capabilities are registering (`add()`) and removing (`remove()`) listeners; easy to use with straightforward method calls for event handling.
- [Lynx: cancelResourcePrefetch() static method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-cancel-resource-prefetch): Cancels resource preloading started by requestResourcePrefetch; takes data and callback params; details cancel status in callback; simple to use with clear parameter and return structures.
- [Lynx: stopExposure() static method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-stop-exposure): Stops exposure detection; no future exposure/anti - exposure events; can restart with resumeExposure; simple method with clear parameter for event sending.
- [Lynx API: getTextInfo()](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-get-text-info): Measures text content width; accepts text string and text attribute info; returns text measurement result with width (px) and line strings; check parameters and note compatibility.
- [Lynx: requireModule() static method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-require-module): Imports modules synchronously like NodeJS `require`; loads remote/local modules; throws exceptions on network/execution/timeout errors, with 5 - second timeout for remote; simple `try - catch` handling.
- [Lynx: resumeExposure() static method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-resume-exposure): Turns on exposure detection; restarts target node visibility detection and may trigger exposure/anti - exposure events; simple grammar with no parameters or return value.
- [Lynx: reload() static method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-reload): Reloads current page like Refresh button; has parameters for new initial data and callback on finish; doesn't trigger frontend `DataProcessor`, preserves certain variables, and triggers component lifecycle callbacks.
- [Lynx API: BeforePublishEvent - add() method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-before-publish-event/before-publish-event-add): Registers listening for an element event; specifies event name, callback, and optional context; simple method with clear parameter definitions.
- [Lynx API: BeforePublishEvent - remove() method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/lynx/lynx-before-publish-event/before-publish-event-remove): Removes listening for an element event; uses `remove(name: string, listener: () => {})` grammar; returns a `BeforePublishEvent` object.
- [NodesRef: fields() method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/nodes-ref/nodes-ref-fields): Query selected node attributes; takes fields (a Record) and callback as params; easy to use with a clear syntax and provides detailed query result handling.
- [Lynx API: NodesRef setNativeProps() method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/nodes-ref/nodes-ref-set-native-props): Directly set selected node attributes; useful for frequent refreshes to avoid performance bottlenecks; use with caution as it's hard to understand. Syntax: setNativeProps(nativeProps: Record<string, any>): SelectorQuery.
- [NodesRef: invoke() method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/nodes-ref/nodes-ref-invoke): Executes UI method of selected node; requires options object with method name, optional params, success & fail callbacks; simple syntax for controlling UI functions.
- [NodesRef: path() method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/nodes-ref/nodes-ref-path): Queries path info from node to page root; uses a callback in syntax to return result and status; simple example for quick understanding.
- [Lynx API: SelectorQuery selectAll() method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/selector-query/selector-query-select-all): Selects all nodes matching a CSS selector under a root node; takes a CSS selector as param, returns a `NodesRef` object; simple method syntax for querying nodes.
- [Lynx API: SelectorQuery select() method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/selector-query/selector-query-select): Selects first matching node; supports CSS selectors (id, class, tag, attribute) and combinators (child, descendant, union); easy to use with clear error handling and compatibility info.
- [SelectorQuery: selectUniqueID() method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/selector-query/selector-query-select-unique-id): Select nodes by specifying `uid`; `uid` is a node's unique id obtained from triggered events; simple method with straightforward parameter and return value.
- [Lynx API: SelectorQuery selectRoot() method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/selector-query/selector-query-select-root): Selects root node in SelectorQuery; returns NodesRef object; simplifies selecting entire page's root element `<page>` for operations like setting native props.
- [SelectorQuery: exec() method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/selector-query/selector-query-exec): Executes all submitted operations; triggers node search and UI operations; simple syntax with no parameters or return value.
- [Lynx API: IntersectionObserver relativeTo() method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/intersection-observer/intersection-observer-relative-to): Specify reference node by id; scale reference node viewport via margins; returns IntersectionObserver object; easy to understand parameter and return value definitions.
- [Lynx API: IntersectionObserver observe() method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/intersection-observer/intersection-observer-observe): Defines target node and callback by id; has `selector` for target node search, `callback` for intersection status change; simple method grammar and parameter specification.
- [IntersectionObserver: relativeToScreen() method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/intersection-observer/intersection-observer-relative-to-screen): Specify screen as reference node; scale reference node's viewport via `margins`; simple, clear grammar for intersection state detection.
- [Lynx API: IntersectionObserver relativeToViewport() method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/intersection-observer/intersection-observer-relative-to-viewport): Specify a LynxView as reference node; scale reference node's viewport via margins; simple method with clear parameter and return value descriptions.
