- [IntersectionObserver: disconnect() method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/intersection-observer/intersection-observer-disconnect): Clears target node and callback; target node stops being observed and callback won't trigger; simple method with no parameters or return value.
- [Lynx API: TouchEvent](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/event/touch-event): Represents touch events on touch surfaces; has instance properties like `detail`, `touches`, `changedTouches`; includes types like `touchstart`, `tap`, `longpress` for easy finger interaction handling.
- [API: Lynx - GlobalEvent](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/event/global-event): Represents a global event object passed between elements/components/client - front end; Core capabilities include listening to events like `keyboardstatuschanged`, `onWindowResize`, `exposure`, `disexposure`; Easy to listen to events via `GlobalEventEmitter`.
- [API: AnimationEvent](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/event/animation-event): Represents animation event object; contains instance property `detail` with animation type, name, and `new_animator`; has various event types for keyframe & transition animation states.
- [Lynx API: Event](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/event/event): Represents event object on trigger; includes types like Touch, Custom, Animation events; offers instance props (type, timestamp, target etc.) and methods (stopPropagation, stopImmediatePropagation) for handling events.
- [Lynx API: CustomEvent](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/event/custom-event): Represents custom event object for component customization state changes; has instance properties `params` (deprecated) and `detail` for state info; simple to understand component state changes.
- [Lynx API: setInterval() global function](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/global/set-interval): Repeatedly calls a function or code fragment at fixed intervals; has `function` and `delay` params, returns timer ID; straightforward syntax with clear parameter descriptions.
- [Lynx API: setTimeout() global function](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/global/set-timeout): Sets a timer to execute a function; specifies delay in milliseconds; returns timer ID for cancellation; simple and standard syntax.
- [Lynx API: clearInterval() global function](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/global/clear-interval): Cancels timer set by `setInterval`; has specific `timerId` parameter from `setInterval`; clear distinction from `clearTimeout` for code clarity.
- [Lynx API: SystemInfo](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/global/system-info): Plain JavaScript object holding system information; provides engine, OS, device pixel, and platform details; simple access to various system - related properties.
- [Lynx API: fetch](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/global/fetch): Used to fetch resources; supports `resource` (string/URL object/`Request` object) and `options` (`RequestInit` object); client env has differences from Web in CORS, streaming, etc.
- [Lynx API: clearTimeout() global function](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/global/clear-timeout): Cancels timer set by `setTimeout`; has specific `timerId` parameter syntax; use for clarity instead of `clearInterval()`.
- [Lynx API: PerformanceMetric](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/performance-api/performance-metric): Describes type for performance metric; includes name, value, start/end timestamps; supports multiple metric types like FCP, TTI, ActualFMP; precise timestamp and value representation.
- [Lynx API: PerformanceObserver](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/performance-api/performance-observer): Observe Lynx Engine performance events; core methods are `observe()` to start and `disconnect()` to stop; created via `lynx.performance.createObserver()` with callback.
- [Lynx API: PerformanceEntry](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/performance-api/performance-entry): Base interface for performance event data; captures container init, page load, and rendering times; supports entry types like `init`, `metric`, `pipeline` with corresponding subclasses.
- [Lynx API: FrameworkPipelineTiming](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/performance-api/framework-pipeline-timing): Describes performance data of key framework rendering stages; Defines instance properties like dsl, stage, and various timestamps; Offers clear property descriptions for easy understanding.
- [Lynx API: PerformanceObserver: disconnect() method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/performance-api/performance-observer/disconnect): Stops receiving performance events; has no parameters or return value; simple method call to disable further event reception.
- [Lynx API: PerformanceObserver: observe() method](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/performance-api/performance-observer/observe): Specifies performance event types to observe; uses entryType or entryType.name as identifiers; recommended to call during initialization for timely data capture.
- [Lynx API: InitBackgroundRuntimeEntry](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/performance-api/performance-entry/init-background-runtime-entry): Provides key moments of Background Thread Runtime initialization; Inherits from `PerformanceEntry`; properties like `loadCoreStart` and `loadCoreEnd` give init timestamps.
- [API: InitLynxviewEntry](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/performance-api/performance-entry/init-lynxview-entry): Provides key initialization times for LynxView; Inherits from PerformanceEntry; Offers properties like entryType, name, createLynxStart/End for detailed metrics.
- [Lynx API: InitContainerEntry](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/performance-api/performance-entry/init-container-entry): Documents container prep work before Lynx page display; defines init - related metrics like openTime, containerInitStart/End; simple example to retrieve InitContainerEntry.
- [Lynx API: LoadBundleEntry](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/performance-api/performance-entry/load-bundle-entry): Records performance data of LoadBundle rendering pipeline; Inherits from PipelineEntry; Provides timestamps for various stages like loading, parsing, and rendering, and framework pipeline timing data.
- [Lynx API: MetricFcpEntry](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/performance-api/performance-entry/metric-fcp-entry): Measures first - screen rendering time; provides timing info for FCP metric with three metrics (`fcp`, `lynxFcp`, `totalFcp`); offers clear property definitions.
- [Lynx API: MetricTtiEntry](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/performance-api/performance-entry/metric-tti-entry): Measures page interactivity time; provides `tti`, `lynxTti`, `totalTti` metrics with different starting points; clear instance properties for easy understanding.
- [Lynx API: PipelineEntry](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/performance-api/performance-entry/pipeline-entry): Records Lynx Pipeline performance data; captures data for Framework Rendering and Pixel Pipeline; offers clear example and well - defined instance properties for easy performance tracking.
- [Lynx API: MetricActualFmpEntry](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-api/performance-api/performance-entry/metric-actual-fmp-entry): Measures time for "real data" page rendering; provides timing info on actual FMP metric; easy to generate metric using lynx pipeline and timing flag.
- [Lynx: TemplateBundle](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-native-api/template-data): Lynx's data structure for runtime data; can be constructed from string or map; core capabilities include methods like fromMap/fromString on Android, initWithDictionary/initWithJson on iOS, and markState for data pre - processing on both platforms.
- [Lynx Native API: Lynx - View](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-native-api/lynx-view): No description provided; unable to determine core capabilities; no ease - of - use statement found.
- [Lynx: LynxService API](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-native-api/lynx-service): Provides Lynx Engine with host - specific capabilities like image, log, FetchAPI; features dependency - separation design; easy to access default implementation or customize according to needs.
- [Lynx Native API: TemplateBundle](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-native-api/template-bundle): Output of Lynx SDK's PreDecode; for Android and iOS; core capabilities include parsing template binary, getting extra info, validating, getting error messages, generating js code cache, and releasing native memory; methods clearly defined for easy use.
- [API: LynxMetaData](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-native-api/lynx-load-meta): Metadata for LynxView to load templates; specify template content, initial data, and configs; clear parameter descriptions and construction methods for Android and iOS.
- [API: LynxUpdateMeta](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-native-api/lynx-update-meta): Metadata structure for LynxView to update template data; specifies parameters like template data and globalProps; easy to construct on Android and iOS platforms.
- [Lynx Native API: updateFontScale](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-native-api/lynx-view/update-font-scale): Changes font scaling ratio in client settings to auto - change text size; has Android and iOS syntax; easy method call for font scale update.
- [Lynx Native API: findViewByIdName](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-native-api/lynx-view/find-view-by-name): Find `UIView` by `name` attribute; has Android (`findViewByName`) and iOS (`viewWithName`) syntax; straightforward method for view lookup in Lynx Native API.
- [Lynx Native API: setExtraTiming](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-native-api/lynx-view/set-extra-timing): Supplements Lynx page load key performance data; includes page open time, TemplateBundle & container timings; has clear syntax for Android & iOS.
- [Lynx - LynxView: LoadTemplate](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-native-api/lynx-view/load-template): Main entrance for clients to load Lynx templates using LynxLoadMeta; provides Android and iOS syntax with meta parameter for template - loading metadata; clear syntax for different platforms.
- [Lynx Native API: findViewByIdSelector](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-native-api/lynx-view/find-view-by-idselector): Find `UIView` using `idSelector`; available in Android (`findUIByIdSelector`) and iOS (`uiWithIdSelector`); simple syntax for specifying view by id.
- [Lynx Native API: Lynx - View destroy](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-native-api/lynx-view/destroy): Releases LynxView memory post - unavailability; has Android syntax for method call; simple memory leak prevention via method call.
- [Lynx Native API: removeLynxViewClient](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-native-api/lynx-view/remove-lynx-view-client): Removes `LynxView` lifecycle event monitoring; has Android syntax with a `client` parameter; straightforward method for unregistering lifecycle event callbacks.
- [Lynx Native API: findUIByIdSelector](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-native-api/lynx-view/find-ui-by-idselector): Locates LynxUI nodes via idSelector; available in Android and iOS with specific syntax; straightforward method for retrieving UI nodes by ID.
- [Lynx Native API: addLynxViewClient](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-native-api/lynx-view/add-lynx-view-client): Register lifecycle event observer for LynxView; supports Android and iOS with respective syntax; easy to implement lifecycle callbacks.
- [Lynx Native API: sendGlobalEvent](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-native-api/lynx-view/send-global-event): Send global events from client to front end; has Android and iOS syntax; easy to use with clear parameter definitions for both platforms.
- [Lynx Native API: LynxView UpdateData](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-native-api/lynx-view/update-data): Main entrance for client to update `LynxView` template data; supports Android and iOS syntax; uses `LynxUpdateMeta` for specifying update data.
- [Lynx Native API: updateViewport](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-native-api/lynx-view/update-viewport): Client updates LynxView view size; has Android and iOS syntax with corresponding parameters; easy method to adjust viewport dimensions.
- [Lynx Native API: findUIByName](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-native-api/lynx-view/find-ui-by-name): Locates LynxUI nodes via name attribute; available in Android and iOS with respective syntax; easy to use with clear parameter definition.
- [Lynx: LynxTemplateResourceFetcher](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-native-api/resource-fetcher/TemplateResourceFetcher): Implements resource loading for Lynx bundles; core method is `fetchTemplate` for getting bundle contents; requires implementation and injection in LynxViewBuilder on Android and iOS.
- [LynxMediaResourceFetcher](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-native-api/resource-fetcher/MediaResourceFetcher): Injected into LynxEngine for path redirection of Image & 3rd - party resources; Core capabilities include redirecting URLs, checking local resources, fetching images; Easy to inject in LynxViewBuilder for Android and iOS.
- [API: LynxGenericResourceFetcher](https://lynxjs.org/api/lynx-native-api/resource-fetcher/GenericResourceFetcher): Implements general resource - loading in LynxEngine; core capabilities include fetching resource content, path, and streaming; easy to inject into LynxView using LynxViewBuilder.
- [API: Error Codes](https://lynxjs.org/api/errors/error-code): Lists error codes; details causes and fix suggestions for errors like app bundle loading, background thread script execution, resource loading; clear error descriptions and fix guidance.
- [API: LynxError](https://lynxjs.org/api/errors/lynx-error): Represents errors with properties like code, subcode, level, and message; offers methods to get error code, subcode, check fatality, and retrieve error info on Android and iOS; straightforward for developers to troubleshoot.
- [API: Element API](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api): Introduces Element PAPI exposed to front - end framework; details API functions and capability boundaries; helps developers build front - end framework and plan API iteration.
- [API: `__GetTag`](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__GetTag): Retrieves the tag selector of an Element node; has a defined syntax with an `element` parameter; easy to use with a straightforward syntax for getting tag selectors.
- [`__GetClasses` API](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__GetClasses): Retrieves all class selectors of an Element; returns them in an array; simple one - parameter syntax for easy use with any Element object.
- [API: `__UpdateComponentID`](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__UpdateComponentID): Updates the componentID of a ComponentElement; takes an element and a case - sensitive componentID as parameters; straightforward syntax with no return value.
- [API: `__CreateElement`](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__CreateElement): Creates an Element object; takes tagName, parentComponentUniqueID, and optional info; returns `base::scoped_refptr<Element>` for frontend to manage element lifecycle.
- [`__ReplaceElements` API](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__ReplaceElements): Replaces a list of Element nodes with specified nodes; key capabilities include batch - replacing child nodes of a parent; easy to use with straightforward syntax and parameter structure.
- [`__NextElement` API](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__NextElement): Retrieves following sibling node; takes an Element object as param, returns next node or undefined; simple syntax for easy node traversal.
- [`__AddInlineStyle` in Element - API](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__AddInlineStyle): Adds inline styles to Element object; takes element, style key ID, and value as params; simple syntax for style addition and removal.
- [`__GetDataByKey` API](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__GetDataByKey): Retrieves data - attribute value of an Element; has `element` and `key` as parameters; simple function syntax for easy data retrieval.
- [`__ReplaceElement`](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__ReplaceElement): Replaces an Element node with a specified node; has `newChild` and `oldChild` parameters; simple syntax for element replacement.
- [API: `__CreateComponent`](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__CreateComponent): Creates a ComponentElement object; has parameters like parentComponentUniqueID, componentID, cssID; returns the ComponentElement object.
- [`__AddDataset` API](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__AddDataset): Adds data- attribute to Element object without affecting existing ones; has parameters element, key, value; easy to use for generating render directives in frontend frameworks.
- [API: `__SetInlineStyles`](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__SetInlineStyles): Sets inline styles for an Element node; takes an Element object and string/object for new styles; simple syntax for overriding inline styles.
- [API: `__AddEvent`](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__AddEvent): Adds an Event Listener to the Element object; takes element, event type, name, and listener as params; simple syntax for adding or removing event listeners.
- [API: `__GetInlineStyles`](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__GetInlineStyles): Retrieves inline styles of an Element; takes an Element object as param and returns inline styles as a map; simple and straightforward function call.
- [API: `__RemoveElement`](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__RemoveElement): Removes an Element node from its parent's child nodes; has specific syntax with `parent` and `child` parameters; easy to use with clear parameter and return value definitions.
- [API: `__SetCSSId`](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__SetCSSId): Sets a CSS ID for an Element node; takes an Element object and a number as parameters; simple syntax for quick ID assignment.
- [API: `__SetDataset`](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__SetDataset): Sets data- attributes for an Element node; takes an Element object and a map dataset as parameters; simple and straightforward syntax for data - set manipulation.
- [API: `__SwapElement`](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__SwapElement): Swaps positions of two Elements; has `childA` and `childB` as parameters with no return value; simple syntax for position - swapping in frontend frameworks.
- [`__AppendElement` API](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__AppendElement): Inserts an Element object after last child of current Element; takes parent and child Element as parameters, returns appended child; simple syntax for element insertion.
- [`__LastElement` API](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__LastElement): Retrieves the last child node of an Element; has a simple syntax with one `element` parameter; straightforward usage demonstrated by example.
- [API: `__GetElementConfig`](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__GetElementConfig): Retrieves all configurations of an Element node; returns config as a map; simple syntax with one parameter (Element object).
- [API: `__GetChildren`](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__GetChildren): Retrieves all child nodes of an Element; has a simple syntax with one parameter (Element object); returns an array of child nodes.
- [`__SetConfig` in Element API](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__SetConfig): Sets configuration for a specific Element node; takes an Element object and a config map as parameters; simple syntax with no return value.
- [API: `__AddConfig`](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__AddConfig): Adds a config key and value to Element object; takes Element, key type string, and any value as params; easy for developers to set custom configs for frontend framework extensions.
- [`__GetEvents` API](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__GetEvents): Retrieves all event listeners of an Element; has simple syntax taking an Element object as param and returning an array of event listener maps; easy to use in frontend frameworks to access element event listeners.
- [`__SetAttribute` API](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__SetAttribute): Sets an attribute for an Element node; has `element`, `key`, `value` params with no return value; straightforward syntax for setting element attributes.
- [API: `__GetEvent`](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__GetEvent): Retrieves event listener specific to an Element; takes element, event name, and type as parameters; simple function call syntax for easy retrieval.
- [`__GetParent` in Element API](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__GetParent): Retrieves the parent node of an Element; has a simple syntax taking an Element object as param; easy to use for retrieving parent nodes in frontend frameworks.
- [`__ElementIsEqual`](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__ElementIsEqual): Determines if two passed Element objects are the same; has syntax `__ElementIsEqual(left: Element, right: Element) : bool`; easy to use with clear parameter and return value descriptions.
- [API: `__SetClasses`](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__SetClasses): Sets class selectors for an Element node; takes an Element object and string of class names as params, overwrites existing classes; simple syntax for setting classes.
- [API: `__FirstElement`](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__FirstElement): Retrieves first child node of an Element; has `element` param of type Element object; simple syntax for easy retrieval of first child Element.
- [API: `__AddClass`](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__AddClass): Adds a class selector to an Element object without altering existing ones; has a simple `__AddClass(element, className)` syntax; easy to use for adding classes in JavaScript code.
- [`__CreatePage` API](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__CreatePage): Creates a PageElement object; takes componentID, cssID, and optional info parameters; simple and straightforward function call syntax.
- [`__GetAttributes` API](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__GetAttributes): Retrieves all attributes of an Element as a map; has a simple syntax with one parameter; easy to use for frontend frameworks to access element attributes.
- [API: `__GetID`](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__GetID): Retrieves Element ID selector; takes an Element object as parameter, returns ID selector or empty string if not set; simple syntax for ID retrieval.
- [API: __SetEvents](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__SetEvents): Sets event listeners for an Element node; takes an Element object and an array of listener maps as parameters; easy to use for generating render instructions with event bindings.
- [`__GetElementUniqueID` API](https://lynxjs.org/api/engine/element-api/__GetElementUniqueID): Retrieves an Element's unique ID; has a simple `__GetElementUniqueID(element: Element) : number` syntax; easy to use for getting element's unique ID in LynxView.
- [Rspeedy: CssLoaderModules](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.cssloadermodules): Manages CSS Modules behavior; enables/disables CSS Modules, has options for auto - enable, export style, class name format, named export; simple interface for module configuration.
- [Rspeedy: PluginReactLynxOptions.customCSSInheritanceList](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/react-rsbuild-plugin.pluginreactlynxoptions.customcssinheritancelist): Controls inheritable CSS properties; sets custom inheritable properties when CSS inheritance is enabled; easy to customize with an array of property names.
- [Rspeedy: Dev.assetPrefix](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.dev.assetprefix): Sets URL prefix for static assets in development; similar to Rspack's output.publicPath but dev - only, auto - appends trailing `/`, sets env var; easy to configure with boolean, string, or placeholder.
- [Rspeedy: ChunkSplitBySize.maxSize](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.chunksplitbysize.maxsize): Defines maximum chunk size in bytes; default is `Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY`; easily set in configuration for chunk - splitting strategy.
- [Rspeedy: Config.environments](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.config.environments): Sets output environment; useful for differentiating Lynx and Web entries, or building Web - only outputs; no complex setup for non - Lynx - for - Web use.
- [Rspeedy: Server](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.server): Changes dev - server behavior; has optional properties for adding headers, specifying host and port; helps configure server - related settings.
- [@lynx-js/react-rsbuild-plugin: enableParallelElement](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/react-rsbuild-plugin.pluginreactlynxoptions.enableparallelelement): Enables Threaded Element Resolution; a boolean property in PluginReactLynxOptions; straightforward property usage for element resolution control.
- [Rspeedy: Output.copy](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.output.copy): Used for copying files to dist directory; supports specifying source and destination paths; examples provided for easy implementation.
- [Rspeedy: Server.host](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.server.host): Property to specify the host for Rspeedy Server to listen to; allows setting host as a string or leaving it undefined; straightforward property for host configuration.
- [Rspeedy: ChunkSplit](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.chunksplit): Configures chunk splitting strategy; allows specifying custom Rspack config and choosing from strategies like 'all - in - one', 'split - by - module'; clearly defined interface with optional properties.
- [Rspeedy: Output.filename](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.output.filename): Determines JavaScript bundle file name; output written to directory specified by output.path; can be set as a string or Filename type for flexible naming.
- [RSpeedy: PluginReactLynxOptions.enableCSSInheritance](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/react-rsbuild-plugin.pluginreactlynxoptions.enablecssinheritance): Enables default CSS inheritance properties; includes properties like direction, color, font - related, text - related; recommended use with customCSSInheritanceList for performance.
- [Rspeedy: Output.distPath](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.output.distpath): Set the directory of dist files; used to configure dist file location in Rspeedy projects; simple configuration via `output.distPath` object.
- [Rspeedy: CssExtractRspackLoaderOptions](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.cssextractrspackloaderoptions): Interface for CSS extract loader options; defines optional `esModule` property affecting JS module syntax; clarifies relation to mini - css - extract - plugin's `esModule` option.
- [Rspeedy: Source.decorators](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.source.decorators): Configures decorators syntax; used to manage decorator - related settings; simple property for decorator configuration.
- [Rspeedy: CssExtract.loaderOptions](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.cssextract.loaderoptions): Defines options of CSS extract loader; used to configure how CSS is extracted; straightforward property for loader configuration.
- [@lynx - js/react - rsbuild - plugin: enableRemoveCSSScope](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/react-rsbuild-plugin.pluginreactlynxoptions.enableremovecssscope): Controls CSS scope usage; true makes all CSS global, false makes all CSS scoped, undefined differentiates CSS Modules; straightforward boolean/undefined - based configuration.
- [Rspeedy: DistPath.root](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.distpath.root): Represents the root directory of all output files in Rspeedy; can be set or left with default 'dist'; simple property for output root configuration.
- [Rspeedy: PluginReactLynxOptions.debugInfoOutside](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/react-rsbuild-plugin.pluginreactlynxoptions.debuginfooutside): Controls placement of debug info outside template; recommended to set to true for reduced template size; simple boolean property configuration.
- [RSpeedy: react-rsbuild-plugin](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/react-rsbuild-plugin): A rsbuild plugin integrating with ReactLynx; creates a rsbuild plugin via `pluginReactLynx` function; provides options interface for the function.
- [Rspeedy: DistPath.css](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.distpath.css): Represents output directory of CSS style files; has default value same as DistPath.intermediate; simple property for CSS output path config.
- [Rspeedy: Dev.watchFiles](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.dev.watchfiles): Watches specified files/directories for changes; can trigger page reload or dev server restart; easy to configure with paths and chokidar options.
- [Rspeedy: ChunkSplitBySize.minSize](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.chunksplitbysize.minsize): Defines minimum chunk size in bytes; default value is 10000; easily configured in performance.chunkSplit settings.
- [@lynx-js/react-rsbuild-plugin: defaultDisplayLinear](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/react-rsbuild-plugin.pluginreactlynxoptions.defaultdisplaylinear): Controls if default CSS 'display' is 'linear'; if false, default is 'flex'; simple boolean configuration.
- [Rspeedy: Filename.template](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.filename.template.md): Obsolete property for template file names; use `Filename.bundle` instead; supports `[name]` and `[contenthash]` placeholders in naming.
- [Rspeedy: TransformImport.transformToDefaultImport](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.transformimport.transformtodefaultimport): Determines conversion of import statements to default imports; shows input/output examples for true/false/undefined values; simple boolean - based configuration.
- [Rspeedy: Filename](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.filename): Determines names of output JavaScript bundle files; sets names for CSS, font, image, and other file types; easy specification of file names via interface properties.
- [Rspeedy: ExposedAPI.version](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.exposedapi.version): Represents the version of Rspeedy; part of Rspeedy's ExposedAPI; simple access to version information as a string.
- [Rspeedy: Config.provider](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.config.provider): Alpha - preview property for Rsbuild provider; allows switching from Rspack to Webpack; easy configuration via `defineConfig`.
- [Rspeedy: Config.server](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.config.server): Controls dev - server behavior; a `Server` or `undefined` can be set; simple property for adjusting server - related settings.
- [Rspeedy: DistPath Interface](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.distpath): Set dist files directory; has optional properties for CSS, JavaScript, and intermediate files' output directories; easy to configure dist file locations.
- [Rspeedy: EntryDescription](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.entrydescription): Describes an entry for projects with multiple entries; Similar to Rspack's Entry Description Object, but Lynx has limited supported properties; includes 'import' and 'publicPath' properties for entry module and resource loading paths.
- [Rspeedy: CreateRspeedyOptions.rspeedyConfig](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.createrspeedyoptions.rspeedyconfig): Represents the Rspeedy config; used to configure Rspeedy settings; part of CreateRspeedyOptions in Rspeedy API.
- [RSpeedy: Minify](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.minify): Configures code minification in production build; controls CSS and JavaScript minification; offers optional minimizer options for easy build optimization.
- [API: Rspeedy Tools](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.tools): Interface to change building tools' behavior; controls options of Rspack, css - loader, Rsdoctor, etc.; provides fine - grained configuration for building tools.
- [Rspeedy: Config Interface](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.config): Configuration for Rspeedy; controls development, output, performance, etc.; provides clear property descriptions for easy setup.
- [Rspeedy: Tools.rsdoctor](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.tools.rsdoctor): Controls Rsdoctor options; allows configuration like disabling client - server; simple to configure via RsdoctorRspackPluginOptions.
- [Rspeedy: EntryDescription.import](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.entrydescription.import): Defines path to entry module(s); if not provided, defaults to `src/index.js`; simple property definition for module import path.
- [@lynx - js/rspeedy: RspeedyInstance](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.rspeedyinstance): Represents the instance of Rspeedy; has method to get Rspeedy configuration; clearly defined in TypeScript.
- [RsPeedy Package: Configuration](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy): All configurations of `@lynx - js/rspeedy`; functions like `createRspeedy`, `defineConfig`, `loadConfig`; offers TypeScript intellisense via `defineConfig`.
- [Rspeedy: Output.cleanDistPath](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.output.cleandistpath): Determines if output dir files are removed before build; default behavior varies by output dir location; easy to configure cleaning for different build scenarios.
- [Rspeedy: PluginReactLynxOptions.jsx](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/react-rsbuild-plugin.pluginreactlynxoptions.jsx.md): Controls JSX transformation; part of `PluginReactLynxOptions`; can be set as `Partial<JsxTransformerConfig>` or `undefined`.
- [Rspeedy: rspackVersion](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.rspackversion): Represents the rspack version; a string variable indicating the version of rspack used; automatically generated by API Documenter.
- [Rspeedy: pluginReactLynx](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/react-rsbuild-plugin.pluginreactlynx): Creates a rsbuild plugin for ReactLynx; takes optional user options; easy to integrate into rsbuild config.
- [Rspeedy: CssLoader](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.cssloader): Controls css - loader options; manages importLoaders and modules properties; default options are preset for common use cases.
- [Rspeedy: Source.define](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.source.define): Define values/expressions at compile - time; supports string as code fragment, object key - based definition, value stringification; useful for environment variables and typeof expressions.
- [Rspeedy: Server.port](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.server.port): Property to specify Rspeedy Server's listening port; defaults to 3000, increments if occupied; easy to set custom port in configuration.
- [Rspeedy: Tools.swc](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.tools.swc): Controls options of builtin:swc-loader; enables configuration of swc - related settings; provides ability to manage swc options in Rspeedy.
- [Rspeedy: Version](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.version): Holds the version number of the Rspeedy library; used to identify the specific release; provides a simple way to access versioning information.
- [Rspeedy: DistPath.jsAsync](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.distpath.jsasync): Defines output dir for async JavaScript files; default is 'async' sub - dir of DistPath.js; simple property for specifying async JS output location.
- [Rspeedy: PluginQRCodeOptions](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/qrcode-rsbuild-plugin.pluginqrcodeoptions.md): Options for pluginQRCode(); allows customization of generated schema via 'schema' property; designed for straightforward option configuration for QR code plugin.
- [Rspeedy: ExposedAPI.exit](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.exposedapi.exit): Allows exiting the process; core capability is to terminate the process with an optional exit code; straightforward method call for process termination.
- [Rspeedy: PluginReactLynxOptions.enableNewGesture](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/react-rsbuild-plugin.pluginreactlynxoptions.enablenewgesture): Enables the new gesture system; a boolean property; easy to configure within plugin options.
- [Rspeedy: Decorators](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.decorators): Configure decorators syntax; specify decorator syntax version ('legacy' or '2022-03'); part of @lynx-js/rspeedy API.
- [Rspeedy: Decorators.version](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.decorators.version): Specify decorator syntax version; can choose 'legacy' or '2022 - 03'; clarifies differences between decorator versions for easy configuration.
- [Rspeedy: Output Minify](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.output.minify): Configures code minification in production build; can enable/disable or set minimizer options; simple configuration via boolean or Minify object.
- [Rspeedy: Output.assetPrefix](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.output.assetprefix): Sets URL prefix for static assets; only affects production build, auto - appends trailing `/`, writes to `process.env.ASSET_PREFIX`; simple to configure in `defineConfig`.
- [Rspeedy: CssLoader.modules](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.cssloader.modules): Enables/disables CSS Modules; set to `false` for performance, `() => true` to enable for all files, or an object for more config; easy to configure based on needs.
- [Rspeedy: defineConfig](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.defineconfig): Helper for TypeScript intellisense; takes Rspeedy config as input, returns identical config; simplifies config setup with autocompletion in `lynx.config.ts`.
- [Rspeedy: TransformImport.libraryDirectory](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.transformimport.librarydirectory): Splice transformed paths; follows rule `${libraryName}/${libraryDirectory}/${member}`; default value is 'lib', easy to configure for import path transformations.
- [Rspeedy: Source.transformImport](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.source.transformimport): Transforms import paths for modular imports from subpaths of third - party packages; similar to babel - plugin - import; enables on - demand component imports with custom config.
- [Rspeedy: loadConfig](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.loadconfig): Function to load build config via config path; takes LoadConfigOptions, returns Promise of LoadConfigResult; simple usage shown in provided example.
- [Rspeedy: Filename.js property](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.filename.js.md): Represents name of JavaScript files in Rspeedy; has default values (`'[name].js'` in dev, `'[name].[contenthash:8].js'` in prod); clearly defined and typed property.
- [Rspeedy: Dev.client](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.dev.client): Configuration of the development client; defines settings for the client in development; straightforward property for client - side development configuration.
- [Rspeedy: ChunkSplitBySize.strategy](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.chunksplitbysize.strategy): Defines ChunkSplitting strategy; options include split - by - experience (default), split - by - module, split - by - size, all - in - one, single - vendor, custom; clearly defined strategy options.
- [Rspeedy: TransformImport.libraryName](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.transformimport.libraryname): Defines original import path for transformation; a required option; straightforward property for specifying import path to transform.
- [API: PluginReactLynxOptions.pipelineSchedulerConfig](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/react-rsbuild-plugin.pluginreactlynxoptions.pipelineschedulerconfig): Composite pipeline scheduling strategies config; manages new strategies like parallel element & batch - rendering; preallocates 64 - bit unsigned int with reserved bits for various pipeline stages.
- [Rspeedy: Filename.image](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.filename.image): Represents name of non - SVG images in Rspeedy; has default value of '[name].[contenthash:8][ext]'; simple property for naming non - SVG images.
- [Rspeedy: CssLoaderModules.namedExport](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.cssloadermodules.namedexport): Controls ES modules named export for locals; shows how different export locals conventions affect accessing CSS class names; simple example code provided.
- [Rspeedy: SourceMap](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.sourcemap): Configures source - map generation for outputs; core property is 'js' to control source map generation method; provides optional settings for fine - tuning source map creation.
- [Rspeedy: CssModules.auto](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.cssmodules.auto): Enables CSS modules automatically based on filenames; supports boolean, RegExp, or function values for custom logic; straightforward configuration via different value types.
- [Rspeedy: Source.alias](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.source.alias): Create aliases for easier module imports; control npm package resolution, ignore modules; simplifies module referencing in configuration.
- [Rspeedy: ChunkSplit.override](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.chunksplit.override): Allows specifying custom Rspack chunk splitting config; enables splitting specific libraries into chunks; straightforward configuration via `override` object.
- [Rspeedy: Tools.cssLoader](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.tools.cssloader): Controls css - loader options; has default options for modules, sourceMap, importLoaders; easy to manage css - loader settings in Rspeedy.
- [Rspeedy: ChunkSplitBySize](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.chunksplitbysize): Interface for configuring chunk splitting strategy; has properties for max and min chunk sizes, override config, and strategy type; clear property descriptions for easy configuration.
- [Rspeedy: CssLoader.importLoaders](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.cssloader.importloaders): Configures loaders for @imported resources and CSS module imports; default values are 1 for CSS files, 2 for Sass/Less files; straightforward configuration via a simple numeric value.
- [Rspeedy: CssExtract](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.cssextract): Controls options of CssExtractRspackPlugin; manages CSS extract loader and plugin options; simplifies configuration of CSS extraction in RSpeedy.
- [Rspeedy: CssModules.exportGlobals](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.cssmodules.exportglobals): Allows exporting names from global class names for import; controlled by a boolean or undefined value; refer to css - loader#exportGlobals for details.
- [Rspeedy: Server.headers](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.server.headers): Adds headers to all responses; allows setting headers in server configuration object; simple to configure via object definition.
- [Rspeedy: CustomizedSchemaFn](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/qrcode-rsbuild-plugin.customizedschemafn): Customize the generated schema; used to customize schema from a given URL to string or object; part of @lynx-js/qrcode-rsbuild-plugin.
- [Rspeedy: CssModules.localIdentName](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.cssmodules.localidentname): Sets format of CSS Modules' compiled className; used to define naming convention for generated class names; refer to css-loader docs for details.
- [Rspeedy: Filename.media](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.filename.media): Represents name of media assets like video; has a `string` or `undefined` type; default value is `'[name].[contenthash:8][ext]'`.
- [Rspeedy: DistPath.js property](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.distpath.js.md): Represents output directory of JavaScript files; default value is 'static/js'; simple property to set output location for JS files.
- [Rspeedy: CssExtractRspackLoaderOptions.esModule](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.cssextractrspackloaderoptions.esmodule): Determines if JS modules use ES modules syntax; like in module concatenation and tree - shaking; default is ES modules, can be set to false for CommonJS.
- [RSpeedy: LoadConfigResult](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.loadconfigresult): Represents result of loadConfig(); has properties configPath (loaded config path) and content (exported config object); clearly defined and type - safe.
- [Rspeedy: DistPath.intermediate](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.distpath.intermediate): Represents the output directory for intermediate files; has an optional string type; default value is `'.rspeedy'`.
- [Rspeedy: Minify.cssOptions](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.minify.cssoptions): Configures CSS minimizer options; allows setting LightningCssMinimizerRspackPluginOptions; simple configuration with example for disabling error recovery.
- [Rspeedy: TransformImport](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.transformimport): Transforms import paths for modular imports from subpaths of third - party packages; enables features like camelCase to kebab - case conversion, custom path transformation; has optional and required properties for configuration.
- [Rspeedy: Output.filenameHash](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.output.filenamehash): Controls adding hash to filename post - production build; supports disabling hash or changing hash format (fullhash, chunkhash, contenthash); simple configuration via defineConfig.
- [Rspeedy: ConsoleType](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.consoletype): Defines the type of console method in Rspeedy; includes types like 'log', 'warn', 'error' etc.; clearly defined for type - safety in code.
- [Rspeedy: Config.dev](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.config.dev): Controls development - related behavior; manages HMR and DevServer; part of the `Config` for easy configuration.
- [Rspeedy: Minify.jsOptions](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.minify.jsoptions): Configures SWC minification options; can disable features like mangle; refer to SwcJsMinimizerRspackPlugin for detailed config.
- [Rspeedy: PluginReactLynxOptions.experimental\_isLazyBundle](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/react-rsbuild-plugin.pluginreactlynxoptions.experimental_islazybundle): An alpha - preview API; generates standalone lazy bundle; intended for developer preview, not for production use.
- [Rspeedy: Performance - removeConsole](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.performance.removeconsole): Controls removal of `console.[methodName]` in production build; can remove all or specific `console` methods; easy configuration via `defineConfig`.
- [Rspeedy: Source.exclude](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.source.exclude): Specify JavaScript files for exclusion from compilation; improves build performance; consistent with Rspack's Rule.exclude for easy config.
- [Rspeedy: Index](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/index): No title or content provided; unable to determine capabilities and ease - of - use statement.
- [Rspeedy: CssLoaderModules.localIdentName](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.cssloadermodules.localidentname): Sets format of CSS Modules' className post - compilation; allows customizing class naming; see css - loader docs for details.
- [Rspeedy: CreateRspeedyOptions.cwd](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.createrspeedyoptions.cwd): Defines the root path of the current build; a crucial property for build configuration; simple to set within the CreateRspeedyOptions object.
- [Rspeedy: LoadConfigResult.content](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.loadconfigresult.content): Represents configuration object from config file; object is already validated; part of LoadConfigResult in @lynx - js/rspeedy.
- [API: PluginReactLynxOptions.firstScreenSyncTiming](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/react-rsbuild-plugin.pluginreactlynxoptions.firstscreensynctiming): Controls when MainThread transfers to Background after first screen; has "immediately" and "jsReady" options; clearly defined flag options.
- [Rspeedy: PluginReactLynxOptions.shake](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/react-rsbuild-plugin.pluginreactlynxoptions.shake): Defines how main - thread code is shaken; a property of PluginReactLynxOptions; part of the @lynx - js/react-rsbuild-plugin API.
- [Rspeedy: Minify.js property](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.minify.js): Determines if JavaScript minification is enabled; can be set to true or false; simple configuration via defineConfig in code.
- [Rspeedy: CSS Modules Configuration](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.cssmodules): Customize CSS Modules configurations; enable for `*.module.css`, `*.module.scss`, `*.module.less`; configure options like `auto`, `exportGlobals`, `exportLocalsConvention`, `localIdentName`.
- [Rspeedy: Filename.font](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.filename.font): Represents the name of font files in Rspeedy; has a default value of '[name].[contenthash:8][ext]'; simple property for font file naming configuration.
- [Rspeedy: LoadConfigOptions](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.loadconfigoptions): Interface for loadConfig options; includes optional 'configPath' and 'cwd' properties; clear definition for loadConfig option configuration.
- [Rspeedy: camelToDashComponentName](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.transformimport.cameltodashcomponentname): Determines conversion of camelCase imports to kebab - case; shows input/output examples; straightforward property to configure import naming.
- [Rspeedy: ExposedAPI.config](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.exposedapi.config): Represents the user config; a fundamental part of the ExposedAPI; straightforward property for setting user - related configurations.
- [Rspeedy: Output.legalComments](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.output.legalcomments): Controls handling of legal comments; options are 'none', 'inline', 'linked'; default is 'none', simple configuration for legal comment handling.
- [Rspeedy: Output.dataUriLimit](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.output.dataurilimit): Sets size threshold for inlining static assets like images and fonts; default is 2kB; easily configured to adjust inlining behavior.
- [Rspeedy: Source.include](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.source.include): Specify additional JavaScript files for compilation; useful for npm packages with ESNext syntax or monorepo libraries; usage consistent with Rspack's Rule.include.
- [Rspeedy: LoadConfigOptions.configPath](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.loadconfigoptions.configpath): Represents an optional `string` property in `LoadConfigOptions`; used to specify a configuration path; part of the typed API for configuration options.
- [Rspeedy: Output.cssModules](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.output.cssmodules): Customize CSS Modules configurations; enable/disable for specific file types; easily customize filtering behavior.
- [Rspeedy: createRspeedy](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.createrspeedy): Creates a Rspeedy instance; Customizes build or dev process in Node.js Runtime; Simple to use with provided example code.
- [Rspeedy: ChunkSplitCustom](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.chunksplitcustom): Interface for configuring chunk splitting strategy; has 'strategy' set to 'custom' and optional 'splitChunks' for custom Rspack config; part of Rspeedy's performance - related configuration.
- [Rspeedy: Config.output](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.config.output): Sets how and where bundles and assets output; core to specifying output for the application; part of the configuration API for precise output control.
- [Rspeedy: PluginReactLynxOptions.enableAccessibilityElement](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/react-rsbuild-plugin.pluginreactlynxoptions.enableaccessibilityelement): Sets default 'accessibility - element' value for <view /> elements; core capability is to manage accessibility settings for view elements; straightforward property for handling accessibility defaults.
- [Rspeedy: RsdoctorRspackPluginOptions](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.rsdoctorrspackpluginoptions): Defines options for RsdoctorRspackPlugin; Represents constructor parameters of RsdoctorRspackPlugin; Technical type definition, no direct ease - of - use statement applicable.
- [Rspeedy: CssModules.exportLocalsConvention](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.cssmodules.exportlocalsconvention): Defines style of exported class names; supports values like 'asIs', 'camelCase', etc.; clear documentation on behavior for each value.
- [Rspeedy: Minify CSS](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.minify.css): Controls CSS minification; in production, uses LightningCssMinimizerRspackPlugin; can customize with @rsbuild/plugin-css-minimizer; easy to enable or disable minification.
- [Rspeedy: Filename.svg](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.filename.svg.md): Represents the name of SVG images; has a default value of '[name].[contenthash:8].svg'; simple property definition for SVG naming.
- [Rspeedy: CssLoaderModules.auto](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.cssloadermodules.auto.md): Allows auto - enabling CSS modules by filenames; supports boolean, RegExp, or function values; easy configuration based on different conditions.
- [Rspeedy: LoadConfigResult.configPath](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.loadconfigresult.configpath): Represents the configuration path that has been loaded; a property of LoadConfigResult; straightforward representation as a string.
- [@lynx - js/react - rsbuild - plugin: removeDescendantSelectorScope](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/react-rsbuild-plugin.pluginreactlynxoptions.removedescendantselectorscope): Used to remove the scope of descendant selectors; part of PluginReactLynxOptions; straightforward boolean - based configuration.
- [RSpeedy: SourceMap.js property](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.sourcemap.js.md): Determines source map generation in RSpeedy; can be set to false to disable, defaults to 'cheap - module - source - map' in dev and false in prod; easy to configure for different environments.
- [Rspeedy: Entry](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.entry): Sets the entry module for the application; supports single entry, single entry with multiple modules, multiple entries with or without EntryDescription; default value is './src/index.js'.
- [Rspeedy: ChunkSplitCustom.splitChunks](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.chunksplitcustom.splitchunks): Allows specifying custom Rspack chunk splitting config; enables splitting specific modules into named chunks like splitting `@lynx-js/react` and `react-router`; easy to configure within `defineConfig`.
- [Rspeedy: Dev.writeToDisk](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.dev.writetodisk): Controls writing dev build artifacts to disk; can be boolean or function for file filtering; bypassed to webpack - dev - middleware for easy build artifact management.
- [Rspeedy: TransformImport.customName](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.transformimport.customname): Customize transformed path; supports formats like kebabCase, snakeCase, camelCase, upperCase, lowerCase; easy to specify path format.
- [Rspeedy: pluginQRCode](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/qrcode-rsbuild-plugin.pluginqrcode): Creates a rsbuild plugin for printing QRCode; takes optional `PluginQRCodeOptions`; easy to integrate in rsbuild config.
- [Rspeedy: EntryDescription.publicPath](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.entrydescription.publicpath): Key option for on - demand - loading and external resource loading; incorrect value causes 404 errors; defines public path for resource loading.
- [Rspeedy: Config.source](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.config.source): Determines behavior of source files; a configurable option in Rspeedy's configuration; simple to adjust based on project needs.
- [Rspeedy: CssExtractRspackPluginOptions.pathinfo](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.cssextractrspackpluginoptions.pathinfo): Defines a property for CssExtractRspackPluginOptions; Indicates whether to include a path in the source map; Simple boolean - type property definition.
- [Rspeedy: Config.tools](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.config.tools): Manages behavior of building tools; allows modification of tool - related options; part of the overall configuration for Rspeedy.
- [Rspeedy: DevClient.websocketTransport](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.devclient.websockettransport): Property representing path to websocket; defaults to `require.resolve('@lynx-js/websocket')`; simple property definition for websocket path.
- [RSpeedy: QRCode - rsbuild - plugin schema](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/qrcode-rsbuild-plugin.pluginqrcodeoptions.schema): Customize generated schema for QRCode plugin; allows single or multiple schema definitions; simple schema customization via function.
- [Rspeedy: CssLoaderModules.exportLocalsConvention](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.cssloadermodules.exportlocalsconvention): Defines style of exported class names; supports 'asIs', 'camelCase', 'camelCaseOnly', 'dashes', 'dashesOnly' values; detailed behavior as per css - loader docs.
- [Rspeedy: DevClient](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.devclient): Configuration of the development client; has an optional property for websocket path; clear property - type and description in the interface.
- [Rspeedy: Dev](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.dev): Controls development - related behavior; manages HMR, DevServer; offers optional asset prefix, client config, file watching, and disk writing control.
- [Rspeedy: Filename.css property](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.filename.css): Represents the name of CSS files in Rspeedy; has a default value of '[name].css'; simple property for CSS file naming configuration.
- [@lynx-js/react-rsbuild-plugin: enableCSSInvalidation](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/react-rsbuild-plugin.pluginreactlynxoptions.enablecssinvalidation): Determines which elements need style recalculation on update; useful when using combinators; can be disabled for better performance if combinators not used.
- [Rspeedy: DistPath.cssAsync](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.distpath.cssasync): Represents the output directory of async JavaScript files; default value is the `async` subdirectory of `DistPath.css`; straightforward property for directory specification.
- [Rspeedy: PluginReactLynxOptions.defineDCE](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/react-rsbuild-plugin.pluginreactlynxoptions.definedce): Similar to 'define' in bundlers; acts at transform time with a DCE pass; allows configuration via Partial<DefineDceVisitorConfig>.
- [Rspeedy: ChunkSplit.strategy](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.chunksplit.strategy): Determines ChunkSplitting strategy; offers options like 'all - in - one', 'split - by - module', 'split - by - experience', 'single - vendor', and custom; simple configuration via defineConfig.
- [Rspeedy: Tools.bundlerChain](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.tools.bundlerchain): Changes Rspack options via rspack-chain; allows customization like setting resolve.fullySpecified; straightforward API for Rspack configuration.
- [Rspeedy: Filename.bundle](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.filename.bundle): Represents name of bundle files; supports placeholders like [name] and [contenthash]; easy to set custom bundle filenames with examples provided.
- [Rspeedy: ExposedAPI.debug](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.exposedapi.debug): Print debug logs; core capability is logging debug messages; straightforward for developers to use in debugging.
- [Rspeedy: ChunkSplitBySize.override](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.chunksplitbysize.override): Allows specification of custom Rspack chunk splitting config; gives control over Rspack's optimization splitChunks config; aids in customizing chunk splitting behavior.
- [Rspeedy: Output](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.output): Sets how and where to output bundles and assets; controls asset prefix, cleaning dist path, file copying, and more; easily configure output - related settings.
- [Rspeedy: ExposedAPI](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.exposedapi): Exposed API for Rsbuild plugins; offers properties like config, debug, exit, logger, and version; used with api.useExposed in Rsbuild plugins.
- [Rspeedy: ExposedAPI.logger](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.exposedapi.logger): Get the Rspeedy logger; a property of ExposedAPI in Rspeedy; straightforward access to the logger.
- [Rspeedy: CssExtractRspackPluginOptions](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.cssextractrspackpluginoptions): Options for CssExtractRspackPlugin; includes optional properties ignoreOrder and pathinfo; clearly defined for easy configuration.
- [Rspeedy: PluginReactLynxOptions.enableICU](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/react-rsbuild-plugin.pluginreactlynxoptions.enableicu): Enables global Intl API; requires checking compatibility with Lynx Share Context; straightforward property configuration.
- [Rspeedy: qrcode - rsbuild - plugin](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/qrcode-rsbuild-plugin): Rsbuild plugin for printing QRCode of template.js url; creates plugin via `pluginQRCode` function; supports customization of generated schema.
- [Rspeedy: LoadConfigOptions.cwd](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.loadconfigoptions.cwd): Represents an optional property in LoadConfigOptions; type is string or undefined; provides flexibility in configuration handling.
- [Rspeedy: CssExtract.pluginOptions](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.cssextract.pluginoptions): Options for CssExtract; related to CssExtractRspackPluginOptions; part of the @lynx - js/rspeedy API.
- [Rspeedy: CssModuleLocalsConvention](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.cssmodulelocalsconvention): Defines style of exported class names in CSS modules; includes 'asIs', 'camelCase', 'camelCaseOnly', 'dashes', 'dashesOnly' options; clarifies behavior for each option.
- [Rspeedy: Performance](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.performance): Configure performance - related settings; manage chunk splitting strategy with chunkSplit; control removal of console statements in production build; provides property - based configuration.
- [Rspeedy: Output.sourceMap](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.output.sourcemap): Configures source - map generation for outputs; determines if and how to generate source - maps; simple boolean or more complex SourceMap configuration available.
- [@lynx - js/react - rsbuild - plugin: enableCSSSelector](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/react-rsbuild-plugin.pluginreactlynxoptions.enablecssselector): Controls new CSS implementation; determines if new CSS implementation is enabled; simple boolean property for configuration.
- [Rspeedy: Config.mode](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.config.mode): Specify Rsbuild and Rspack build mode; default depends on NODE_ENV, overrides NODE_ENV if set; CLI commands set default mode values; easy to configure mode - specific optimizations.
- [Rspeedy: CreateRspeedyOptions](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.createrspeedyoptions): Interface for `createRspeedy` method options; defines optional properties like `cwd` (root build path) and `rspeedyConfig` (Rspeedy config); clearly defined for type - safe usage.
- [Rspeedy: CssExtractRspackPluginOptions.ignoreOrder](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.cssextractrspackpluginoptions.ignoreorder): Property of CssExtractRspackPluginOptions; indicates whether to ignore order; type can be boolean or undefined.
- [Rspeedy: ChunkSplitCustom.strategy](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.chunksplitcustom.strategy): Defines ChunkSplitting strategy; includes options like 'split - by - experience', 'split - by - module', 'split - by - size'; clear documentation of available strategies.
- [Rspeedy: Config.plugins](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.config.plugins): Customize Rspeedy build process; uses plugin APIs from Rsbuild; enables various build customizations.
- [Rsbuild: Source.assetsInclude](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.source.assetsinclude): Define additional static asset file types; defaults to `undefined`; consistent with Rspack's Condition for matching module paths or assets, easy to configure via `source.assetsInclude` in Rsbuild config.
- [RsPeedy: Tools.rspack](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.tools.rspack): Controls Rspack options; supports object config and functions with `env`, `mergeConfig`, `appendPlugins` utils; easy to configure Rspack settings in RsPeedy.
- [API: Rspeedy Source.entry](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.source.entry): Sets the entry module in Rspeedy; can use single or multiple entries, with or without multiple entry modules; default value is `'./src/index.js'`.
- [Rspeedy: PluginReactLynxOptions.targetSdkVersion](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/react-rsbuild-plugin.pluginreactlynxoptions.targetsdkversion): Specifies minimal Lynx SDK version for Lynx template to run; simple property for version - setting; denoted by string type.
- [Rspeedy: Performance.chunkSplit](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.performance.chunksplit): Configures chunk splitting strategy; allows definition of ChunkSplit, ChunkSplitBySize, or ChunkSplitCustom; straightforward property for strategy setup.
- [Rspeedy: Source.tsconfigPath](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.source.tsconfigpath): Configure custom tsconfig.json path; affects Path Aliases and Type Check Plugin; simple path configuration, relative paths resolved relative to project root.
- [Rspeedy: PluginReactLynxOptions](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/react-rsbuild-plugin.pluginreactlynxoptions.md): Options for pluginReactLynx(); controls compatibilities, CSS inheritance, debug info, and various features like ICU, new gesture system; easy to configure with optional properties.
- [Rspeedy: Config.performance](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.config.performance): Used to configure performance options; manages performance - related settings; provides a way to set the [Performance](./rspeedy.performance.md) option.
- [Rspeedy: Tools.cssExtract](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.tools.cssextract): Controls options of CssExtractRspackPlugin; manages CSS extraction settings; part of the Rspeedy Tools API.
- [Rspeedy: Source](https://lynxjs.org/api/rspeedy/rspeedy.source): Changes source file behavior; configures aliases, assets inclusion, decorators, entry, exclusions & inclusions; easy to customize import paths and tsconfig.
- [React API: Directives](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Document.directives): Define thread - specific functions; `'background only'` for background - only code, reducing bundle size; `'main thread'` for main - thread - only code, useful for animations; easy to mark functions for specific threads.
- [React API: useDebugValue()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.useDebugValue): Labels custom hooks in React DevTools; useful for shared library hooks; introduced in React version 16.8.0.
- [React API: InitDataProvider()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.InitDataProvider): Provider component to supply `initData`; wraps JSX; takes `props` and optional `deprecatedLegacyContext`; easy to integrate in React applications.
- [React: useInitDataChanged()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.useInitDataChanged): React Hook for `initData` change notifications; takes a callback function as parameter; simple to implement in React components.
- [React API: Variable root](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Variable.root.md): Represents the default and only root of ReactLynx for rendering JSX; can be imported from `@lynx-js/react`; straightforward import mechanism.
- [React: PureComponent](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Class.PureComponent.md): A React component class; extends `Component`; has lifecycle methods like `componentDidMount`, `shouldComponentUpdate`; simplifies re - render checks with shallow comparison of props and state.
- [React: Function.createElement()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.createElement.md): Creates React elements; supports various element types like HTML, SVG, function components, and class components; provides type - specific overloads for accurate usage.
- [API React: Function withInitDataInState()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.withInitDataInState): Higher - Order Component for React; injects `initData` into class component state, updates state on data change, and removes listener on unmount; simple to use as shown in example.
- [React: useMainThreadRef()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.useMainThreadRef): Hook for creating a `MainThreadRef`; preserves state between main - thread function calls; should be called at component's top - level.
- [React: useLynxGlobalEventListener()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.useLynxGlobalEventListener): Adds global event listeners early in React; takes event name and listener as params; easy way to listen for events like 'exposure' and 'disexposure'.
- [API - React: Function: ~~useLayoutEffect()~~](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.useLayoutEffect): Now an alias of `useEffect`; runs effects after main thread DOM update without blocking; use `useEffect` instead due to imprecise timing for layout reads and re - renders.
- [React: Function: forwardRef()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.forwardRef): Allows component to expose element node to parent via ref; has type parameters for element node and props types; easy to use with well - defined type and parameter requirements.
- [React API: Function.Suspense()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.Suspense): Allows display of fallback until children finish loading; takes `SuspenseProps` as parameter, returns `null` or `ReactElement`; follows React Docs implementation.
- [React: Class MainThreadRef](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Class.MainThreadRef.mdx): A ref for main - thread access only; preserves states between main - thread function calls; allows reading and writing of `current` property data across multiple main - thread functions.
- [React: useReducer()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.useReducer): An alternative to useState; handles complex state logic with multiple sub - values; optimizes performance for deep updates; version 16.8.0.
- [React API: Function.memo()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.memo): Skips re - rendering a component when props are unchanged; Determines prop changes via `propsAreEqual` function; Simplifies component rendering management by preventing unnecessary re - renders.
- [React: Function.useInitData](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.useInitData): React Hook for getting `initData`; triggers re - render when `initData` changes; simple to use as shown in example code.
- [API - React: useSyncExternalStore()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.useSyncExternalStore): React hook for syncing external stores; takes subscribe, getSnapshot, and optional getServerSnapshot; useful for integrating external state management.
- [React: Function - useContext()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.useContext): Retrieves current context value; accepts context object from `React.createContext`; introduced in React version 16.8.0.
- [React API: Built - in Macros](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Document.built-in-macros.md): Processes built - in macro defs like `__BACKGROUND__` and `__MAIN_THREAD__`; controls code execution in background and main threads; automatically removes code blocks with `false` conditions during compilation.
- [@lynx - js/react](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/index): API reference for ReactLynx framework; based on React 17 API, fully compatible with React 17 or Preact; provides various React - like classes, interfaces, functions, and components for Lynx development.
- [React API: Interface: InitDataRaw](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Interface.InitDataRaw.md): Interface for customizing `defaultDataProcessor`; used with `lynx.registerDataProcessors`; enables parameter customization for data processing.
- [React API: Function.createRef](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.createRef): Creates a mutable ref object; type - parameterized for flexibility; simple function call for ref creation.
- [React: Function - useCallback()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.useCallback): Memoizes callbacks; returns a version that changes only if specified inputs change; introduced in React 16.8.0.
- [React: Function.useEffect()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.useEffect): Manages side - effects in React; runs after main - thread DOM update without blocking; accepts an effect function and optional dependencies list for conditional activation.
- [React: runOnMainThread()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.runOnMainThread): Allows async triggering of main - thread functions on main thread; takes a main - thread function as param and returns a function to call with args; straightforward function usage.
- [React API: InitDataConsumer()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.InitDataConsumer.md): A consumer component providing `initData`; should be used with InitDataProvider; takes `props` of type `ConsumerProps<InitData>` and returns `null` or `ReactElement`.
- [React API: Function.createRoot](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.createRoot): Function to create the default and only root of ReactLynx for rendering JSX; returns `Root`; deprecated, use `root` instead.
- [API: React - useImperativeHandle](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.useImperativeHandle): Customizes instance value exposed to parent components via ref; should be used with React.forwardRef; introduced in React version 16.8.0.
- [React API: Interface.DataProcessorDefinition](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Interface.DataProcessorDefinition.md): Definition of DataProcessors; has optional `dataProcessors` for `lynx.registerDataProcessors`, and `defaultDataProcessor` to customize input/output; clearly defined properties for easy implementation.
- [React: runOnBackground()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.runOnBackground): Allows triggering JS functions asynchronously on the JS context; takes a JS function as a parameter and returns a function to trigger it; easy asynchronous function execution.
- [API - React: Class.Component](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Class.Component.md): React's base class for components; manages state and props, has lifecycle methods like `componentDidMount` and `shouldComponentUpdate`; provides a structured way to build reusable UI components.
- [React API: Function - useState()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.useState): React hook that returns a stateful value and an update function; used to add state to functional components; introduced in React version 16.8.0.
- [API: React - Interface: Root](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Interface.Root.md): Default root from `@lynx-js/react` for JSX rendering; allows registering data processors via `registerDataProcessors` and rendering JSX with `render`; straightforward API for basic rendering operations.
- [React API: isValidElement()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.isValidElement): Checks if an object is a valid React element; takes `undefined`, `null`, or an `object` as parameter; easy type - checking for React elements.
- [React: Function - useRef()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.useRef): React's `useRef` function; returns a mutable ref object with a persistent `.current` property; useful for more than `ref` attribute, like keeping mutable values. Version: 16.8.0.
- [API - React: Interface: Lynx](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Interface.Lynx.md): APIs under `lynx` global variable added by ReactLynx; includes `querySelector`, `querySelectorAll`, `registerDataProcessors`, `triggerGlobalEventFromLepus`; clear parameter and return value descriptions.
- [API - React: createContext()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.createContext): Creates a Context for components to provide or read; takes a `defaultValue` as fallback when no matching Provider is above; straightforward function with clear parameter and return types.
- [React: Function.useMemo](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.useMemo): React hook for memoizing values; only recomputes when dependencies change; introduced in React version 16.8.0.
- [React: Function lazy()](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.lazy): Defers component code - loading until first render; takes a function returning a Promise; caches the Promise and its resolved value; follows React's lazy function API.
- [API - React: Function.Fragment](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Function.Fragment): Groups elements without wrapper node; takes `props` object with optional `props.children`; returns `null` or `ReactElement`; simple way to group elements in React.
- [React API: Interface: InitData](https://lynxjs.org/api/react/Interface.InitData): Customize `defaultDataProcessor` return value; Use with `lynx.registerDataProcessors`; Allows for interface extension for data processor customization.


## React

- [React: Routing](https://lynxjs.org/react/routing): Enables single - page routing in React; Configures routes using `<Routes>` and `<Route>`; Navigates with `useNavigate`, accesses URL values like route params via `useParams` and location object via `useLocation`; requires `react-router` v6 installation.
- [React: Rendering Process and Lifecycle](https://lynxjs.org/react/lifecycle): Addresses mobile performance bottlenecks; dual - thread architecture divides tasks for better performance; unique lifecycle and rendering process in Lynx, e.g., async lifecycle hooks in background thread.
- [React: Code Splitting](https://lynxjs.org/react/code-splitting): Enables code splitting in Rspack for performance gains; supports lazy - loading components, standalone projects; simplifies the process of deferring code loading and handling errors.
- [React: What is ReactLynx?](https://lynxjs.org/react/introduction): Official React framework for Lynx; enables native app dev with React mental model; core capabilities include idiomatic React, dual - threaded optimizations, JSX constant folding; easy for React web devs to switch with minor import and component changes.
- [React: Thinking in ReactLynx](https://lynxjs.org/react/thinking-in-reactlynx): Follows React's model with Lynx's dual - threaded runtimes; code runs on main and background threads; clear rules for code execution on each thread for better performance and user - friendly code separation.
- [React: Tutorial: Payment Details](https://lynxjs.org/react/payment-details): Learn advanced Lynx features via a payment details page; build interactive scrolling lists, create 3D animations, and pass data between components; step - by - step implementation guide.
- [React on Lynx](https://lynxjs.org/react/index): Develop Lynx with familiar React; provides an idiomatic way to work with React on Lynx; easy start with provided quick start guide.
- [React: Data Fetching](https://lynxjs.org/react/data-fetching): Retrieves data from external sources in ReactLynx apps; uses Lynx Fetch API, can pair with TanStack Query; simplifies data fetching and state management with TanStack Query's hooks.
- [React: Main Thread Script](https://lynxjs.org/react/main-thread-script): JS script for main - thread execution; addresses Lynx's event response delay in multi - threaded architecture, enables smooth animations and gesture handling; simplifies event synchronization on main thread.
- [React: Zustand State Management](https://lynxjs.org/react/state-management/zustand): Small, fast, and scalable state management; based on React Hooks with a flexible API; easy to install and use with simple examples.
- [React: State Management - Valtio](https://lynxjs.org/react/state-management/valtio): Simple and powerful proxy - based state management; offers fine - grained subscriptions and reactivity; easy to install and use with examples.
- [React: Jotai](https://lynxjs.org/react/state-management/jotai): Atomic approach to global React state management; simple installation, easy to use with examples; provides an intuitive way to manage state in React applications.

## rspeedy

- [Rspeedy: Static Assets](https://lynxjs.org/rspeedy/assets): Supports using static assets like images, fonts, audio, video; Allows import in JS & CSS, has URL prefix config, public folder; Offers inlining options and ability to extend asset types; easy asset handling and configuration.
- [Rspeedy: Plugin](https://lynxjs.org/rspeedy/plugin): Leverages Rsbuild's powerful plugin system for user extension; enables registering hooks, transforming code, modifying configurations; supports Rsbuild, Rspack/Webpack plugins for various tasks.
- [Rspeedy: CLI](https://lynxjs.org/rspeedy/cli): Lightweight CLI for Rspeedy; main commands include `dev`, `build`, `inspect`, `preview`; easy to use with global installation and version selector feature.
- [Rspeedy: Use Rsdoctor](https://lynxjs.org/rspeedy/use-rsdoctor): Build analyzer for Rspeedy projects; visualizes compilation, analyzes build artifacts & times, supports custom rules; easy to enable with simple commands in dev or build.
- [Rspeedy: Output Files](https://lynxjs.org/rspeedy/output): Details output file directory structure; controls output dir for different file types, modifies dir/filename; easy configuration with provided options.
- [Rspeedy: Module Resolution](https://lynxjs.org/rspeedy/resolve): Simplifies module imports with concise paths; enables environment - aware module substitution; easy to define path aliases via `tsconfig.json` or `source.alias` in Rsbuild.
- [Rspeedy: Build Profiling](https://lynxjs.org/rspeedy/build-profiling): Analyze project performance bottlenecks; uses Rsdoctor, Node.js profiling, and Rspack profiling; easy commands for profiling with clear output visualization.
- [Rspeedy: Index](https://lynxjs.org/rspeedy/index): No detailed information provided in the given content; unable to extract capabilities and ease - of - use statement.
- [Rspeedy: Styling](https://lynxjs.org/rspeedy/styling): Offers diverse application styling methods; supports CSS Modules, Global CSS, pre - processors (Sass/Less/Stylus), and PostCSS; easy to manage and configure styles with default setups and minimal additional configs.
- [Rspeedy: Upgrade](https://lynxjs.org/rspeedy/upgrade): Upgrade Rspeedy - related dependencies; use `upgrade-rspeedy` tool to upgrade to latest, specific, or canary versions; simple command - line interface for version upgrades.
- [Rspeedy: TypeScript](https://lynxjs.org/rspeedy/typescript): Rspeedy, powered by Rsbuild, supports TypeScript by default; enables path aliases, custom `tsconfig.json` paths; simplifies TypeScript integration with built - in features and easy setup via `create - rspeedy`.

## blog

- [Lynx: Lynx Blog](https://lynxjs.org/blog/index): Latest Lynx articles and release announcements; features articles like "Lynx: Unlock Native for More"; easy access to Lynx - related news.
- [Lynx: Unlock Native for More](https://lynxjs.org/blog/lynx-unlock-native-for-more): Enables native UIs from single web codebase; offers scale, velocity, and versatility; allows familiar web dev with markup and CSS, and has optimized threading for interactivity.

## Optional

- [Contribute to Lynx](https://github.com/lynx-family/lynx): The github repository to host Lynx development and the guildlines to follow.
